Caption: Vous utilisez ChatGPT pour votre activité immobilière ? Alors voici l’erreur que beaucoup de professionnels commettent encore. Ils demandent simplement : "Rédige-moi une annonce immobilière." ou "Fais-moi un post LinkedIn." Résultat : un texte générique, peu différenciant et rarement adapté à leur cible. L’IA n’est pas magique. La qualité du résultat dépend directement de la qualité de la demande. Par exemple, au lieu de demander : ❌ "Rédige une annonce immobilière." Essayez : ✅ "Rédige une annonce immobilière pour une maison familiale de 120 m² avec jardin, située dans un secteur recherché. Le ton doit être rassurant, professionnel et mettre en avant la qualité de vie du quartier." La différence est immédiate. L’enjeu aujourd’hui n’est pas seulement de savoir utiliser l’IA. C’est surtout de savoir lui donner les bonnes consignes pour obtenir des contenus réellement utiles à votre activité. Chez Les Formateurs Immo, nous accompagnons les professionnels de l’immobilier à intégrer ces nouveaux outils avec une approche concrète et directement applicable sur le terrain. Caption: 🚀💻 **Aprendizaje Inteligente en Programación: Evidencia Científica para la Educación del Futuro** La Inteligencia Artificial está transformando la enseñanza de la programación. Este estudio presenta una revisión sistemática de la literatura que analiza cómo las tecnologías inteligentes están mejorando el aprendizaje de la programación en distintos niveles educativos. 🔍 **Hallazgos principales:** ✔️ **Problema abordado:** La enseñanza de la programación enfrenta desafíos relacionados con la personalización del aprendizaje, la retroalimentación oportuna y el desarrollo de habilidades de resolución de problemas. Las tecnologías basadas en IA surgen como una alternativa prometedora para enfrentar estas limitaciones. ✔️ **Metodología rigurosa:** Mediante el protocolo PRISMA se analizaron **125 estudios científicos** provenientes de **Scopus** e **IEEE Xplore**, seleccionando finalmente **22 investigaciones de alta calidad** publicadas entre 2022 y 2026. ✔️ **Resultados destacados:** 🤖 Los **Sistemas Tutores Inteligentes (ITS)**, los **Modelos de Lenguaje de Gran Escala (LLMs)** como ChatGPT, las plataformas de aprendizaje adaptativo y los entornos gamificados fueron las tecnologías más utilizadas. 📈 Los estudios reportan mejoras significativas en: • Rendimiento académico • Resolución de problemas • Depuración de código • Pensamiento computacional • Motivación y compromiso estudiantil ⚠️ **Desafíos identificados:** Persisten preocupaciones relacionadas con la dependencia excesiva de herramientas de IA, respuestas incorrectas generadas por los modelos, sesgos algorítmicos y la escasez de estudios de largo plazo. 💡 **Conclusión principal:** La Inteligencia Artificial tiene el potencial de revolucionar la educación en programación, pero sus mejores resultados se alcanzan cuando complementa la labor docente y favorece experiencias de aprendizaje más personalizadas, adaptativas y centradas en el estudiante. 📚 Revisión Sistemática de Literatura 🎓 Educación en Programación • Inteligencia Artificial • ChatGPT • Sistemas Tutores Inteligentes • Aprendizaje Adaptativo InteligenciaArtificial Educacion Programacion ChatGPT LLM SmartLearning TecnologiaEduca

Caption: GenUI shows the result. But where does the agent get the data? 🔌 Meet MCP — the Model Context Protocol. The clean mental model: MCP fetches and acts; GenUI builds the interface on top. Agent calls a tool (search_flights) → gets structured JSON → emits an A2UI blueprint (flight cards + Select) → your native app renders it → tap Select → book_flight → loop. Every action is an MCP call; every screen is a GenUI surface. Why now: donated to the Linux Foundation (Dec 2025), ~10k public servers, early-majority adoption. Caption: Your store is closed. The sale isn't. Meta just rolled out Business Agent at Cannes Lions: an AI that replies, recommends products and closes the sale 24/7 inside WhatsApp, Instagram and Messenger DMs. Over 1M businesses are already running it. The shift is real: the highest-intent moment is a customer sliding into your DMs at midnight, and most businesses miss it because nobody is awake to answer. An always-on agent catches that intent, answers the question, and sends the checkout link before the buyer cools off. How to use it without losing your brand voice: 1. Feed it your real catalogue, prices, shipping and returns so answers are accurate. 2. Set the tone and the hard rules (what it can discount, when to hand off to a human). 3. Give it one job per conversation: answer, recommend, then close with a link. 4. Review the transcripts weekly and tighten the gaps. Treat the agent as a junior closer you train, not a black box you trust blindly. Would you let an AI close sales in your DMs? Caption: Insurance claims are not just an operations process. They are where trust is won or lost. When claims move through emails, spreadsheets, old portals, and manual reviews, the business creates hidden risk. That risk shows up as: • slower decisions • poor customer updates • duplicate work • missed recovery opportunities • overpayment • higher support volume • lower retention This is called claims leakage. And it is one of the quietest profit drains in insurance. PwC found that improving claims leakage can often create 5–10% savings. JD Power also found that customers who rate their digital claims experience as poor or just okay are far more likely to leave or not renew. The lesson is simple: Claims modernization is not just about speed. It is about better data, cleaner workflows, smarter routing, clearer updates, and stronger trust. AI can help. Automation can help. Better SaaS platforms can help. But only when the system is built around real insurance workflows. At Zenveus, we help insurance companies build custom software, AI workflows, SaaS platforms, customer portals, and automation systems that make operations faster, cleaner, and easier to scale. Caption: Most IAM reviews focus on individual permissions. But attackers don't think that way. They look for combinations. A permission that appears harmless on its own can become dangerous when combined with another permission elsewhere in the environment. Consider examples like: • KMS Decrypt + S3 Read • Secrets Manager Access + Lambda Update • CloudTrail Stop Logging + IAM Modification • S3 Policy Changes + Bucket Read/Write Individually, these permissions may not trigger major concern. Together, they can create a direct path to data exposure, privilege escalation, or reduced visibility during an attack. This is one of the biggest challenges in cloud security today. Most tools identify individual findings. Few analyze how permissions interact across identities, resources, and services to create hidden attack paths. Securitain was built to address exactly this problem. By analyzing IAM permissions, trust relationships, resource access, and toxic permission combinations, Securitain helps AWS teams uncover risks that may remain hidden in traditional security reviews. Because the real risk is often not a single permission. It's the combination. 🔐 Detect hidden attack paths 🔐 Identify toxic permission combinations 🔐 Prioritize risks based on real impact 🔐 Improve AWS IAM security posture Securitain by Cloudain Making cloud security findings easier to understand, prioritize, and remediate. AWS CloudSecurity IAM CyberSecurity DevSecOps SecurityPosture LeastPrivilege AWSIAM Securitain Cloudain

Caption: Innovation Or Better Marketing? A new AI infrastructure battle is heating up. Databricks recently unveiled its LTAP architecture, calling it the first system to combine transactional and analytical databases into a single copy. SingleStore wasn't impressed. CEO Raj Verma quickly pushed back, arguing that SingleStore has offered the same capability for years and already serves hundreds of customers using it. The company's response was blunt: "We didn't announce it. We shipped it, seven years ago." SingleStore also challenged Databricks' open-source narrative, claiming the architecture creates deeper customer lock-in. Here's what most people miss: In tech, winning isn't always about inventing first. It's often about owning the narrative. Many breakthrough technologies become famous only when a larger player enters the market. Real question: What matters more in technology: being first to build it, or being the company that convinces the world it matters? GenLeaders Databricks SingleStore AIInfrastructure DataInfrastructure

Caption: $6.9 billion in annualized revenue and it’s actually speeding up. Databricks just announced its annualized revenue hit $6.9B, up more than 80% year-over-year from $5.4B last quarter. CEO Ali Ghodsi shared the milestone at the company’s Data and AI Summit. The AI product line alone now runs at $1.7B, up from $1.4B just four months earlier, with net revenue retention holding above 140%. But there’s a twist: gross margins slipped to ~74% from above 80% as AI agents fire off far more queries and compute, and Ghodsi expects them to fall further. At a $134B valuation, Databricks is now worth more than public rival Snowflake. Is shrinking margins the real cost of the agentic AI boom? 👇 Databricks AI enterpriseAI dataanalytics AliGhodsi AIagents

Caption: "AGI is here today. Just not at work." That opening line from the Databricks Data + AI Summit 2026 keynote perfectly framed what stood out most to our Co-Founder, Sandip Chintawar, throughout the event. The biggest takeaway wasn't a single product launch. It was the shift toward making AI useful inside the systems where real business work happens. From Lakehouse//RT enabling real-time analytics on a single copy of data, to Agent Bricks, Genie One, ZeroOps, Lakeflow, CustomerLake, and Omnigent, Databricks unveiled a vision where data, AI, governance, and applications work together as one intelligent platform. The focus is moving beyond building AI models to creating AI systems that understand business context, operate on trusted data, and deliver outcomes at scale. Caption: This is the SQL window function cheat sheet I wish people memorized. Not every function is a random trick. They fall into groups. Number rows: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK. Look around: LAG, LEAD. Calculate without collapsing: SUM, AVG, COUNT, MIN, MAX. Control the window: PARTITION BY, ORDER BY, and frames. That is the real interview pattern. If you can say which group, which order, and which nearby rows matter, most window function questions become simple. Save this before your next SQL interview or analytics PR. Which one still trips you up? HackProduct SQL DataEngineering AnalyticsEngineering InterviewPrep

Caption: ¿Tu negocio crea valor con sus datos o solo quema dinero? 📊🔥 La mayoría de las empresas no sufren por falta de datos. Tienen ventas, clientes, inventario y marketing registrándose 24/7. El verdadero problema es la falta de dirección. He visto demasiados equipos invertir miles de dólares en dashboards complejos, reportes infinitos y herramientas de analítica que al final nadie usa para tomar decisiones. Datos que se acumulan en un data lake o en hojas de cálculo, pero que jamás se traducen en acción. Eso no es gestión de datos. Eso es gasto de datos. 💸 Y ojo, esto se vuelve crítico si tienes en la mira la automatización o la Inteligencia Artificial. El 90% de los proyectos de IA y Machine Learning fracasan por lo mismo: no tener una infraestructura de datos sólida detrás. Sin esa base estructurada, ningún algoritmo (por más avanzado o costoso que sea) te va a dar resultados confiables. Basura entra, basura sale. Un negocio que realmente sabe monetizar y aprovechar su información, responde estas 3 preguntas antes de tocar una sola línea de código o comprar un software: 1️⃣ ¿Qué decisión de negocio quiero mejorar con esta información? 2️⃣ ¿Quién la va a usar en el día a día y para qué exactamente? 3️⃣ ¿Cómo voy a medir el impacto financiero ocn esa decisión? Sin estas respuestas claras, cualquier inversión en infraestructura o analítica es solo ruido caro. 👉 Desliza en el carrusel para ver la diferencia exacta entre los datos que generan ROI y los que solo generan costos en la nube, y los pasos clave para empezar a ordenar tu casa digital. 📩 ¿Quieres auditar cómo están tus datos hoy y dónde está la oportunidad real de optimización en tu negocio? Caption: Two months in. Here is what building with AI actually looks like for one of our engineers. The technical work was real from week one. A Snowflake MCP Gateway deployed on Azure. A product query agent for a retail client where the debugging never really stopped. An order processing pipeline with human review queues, business rule validation, and a conversational interface operators could use in plain language. Then came the work she did not expect. Co-facilitating AI training sessions for enterprise teams. Building a product-specific agent. Now moving into a finance agent where the data is more sensitive, the rules are stricter, and the margin for error is smaller. The lesson that stuck. The model is almost never where it breaks. It breaks at the context. The schema. The structure of what you hand the system before it is asked to do anything. Pratiksha came in from a marketing analytics background. It turned out to be the thing that made her useful fastest. Knowing what a business team actually needs from a tool is not a soft skill in this work. It is the job. Building a system well and explaining it well are completely different skills. Follow to hear more such stories. Topics: AI engineering, production AI, AI agents, enterprise AI

Caption: Trust is usually treated like a brand issue, but in modern marketing it is also tied closely to how well the business is organized behind the scenes. A company can have a good logo, a polished website, and strong visuals, but still create a weak customer experience if the systems behind the marketing are disconnected. You see this when an ad sends people to a page that does not match the offer, when an inquiry form does not trigger the right follow-up, when customer information sits in the wrong place, or when the sales conversation feels disconnected from what the marketing promised. Customers may not see the system behind the business, but they feel the result of it. They feel it when the response is slow, when the next step is unclear, when the message changes from one place to another, or when the business does not feel as organized after the inquiry as it did before. That is why marketing infrastructure matters. The website, CRM, email follow-up, lead handling, advertising, content, automation, and reporting should work together as one connected customer journey. When those pieces are aligned, the business has more control over what happens after someone shows interest. This is where modern marketing systems become valuable. They do not just help a business move faster. They help the business respond more consistently, manage leads more properly, and create a stronger experience from first impression to follow-up. For businesses that want to grow, the goal is not just to generate more attention. The goal is to make sure the system behind the attention is strong enough to support the customer when they are ready to move forward. Graphic direction: Create a LOGIC Marketing Systems static post with the headline “Trust Needs Infrastructure.” Use a modern connected-system visual showing Website, CRM, Email, Ads, Lead Follow-Up, and Reporting linked into one controlled customer journey. Keep the design clean, technical, premium, and business-friendly, with a polished systems architecture feel. Caption: Digital Marketing (SEO, PPC & Lead Generation) A business owner told me last year that digital marketing does not work for his industry. I asked him what he had tried. He ran a few Facebook ads two years ago. Got some clicks. No conversions. Stopped. That was his entire data set. One short experiment, written off as proof. Meanwhile his direct competitor, same city, same industry, had spent eighteen months building a content and SEO strategy that now brings in 60% of their revenue from inbound leads. They did not get lucky. They got consistent. Here is what the data actually says about businesses that invest properly in digital marketing. SEO leads have a 14.6% close rate. Outbound leads average 1.7%. The same sales effort closes at nearly nine times the rate when the prospect found you first instead of the other way around. 70% of B2B buyers have already completed their research before they ever contact a vendor. If your business is not visible during that research phase, you do not lose the deal. You never enter the consideration set at all. Businesses that maintain a consistent content strategy generate 67% more leads per month than those that do not. Not from increasing ad spend. From expertise that works while they sleep. The businesses I see winning are rarely the ones with the single best product in their category. They are the ones prospects find first, trust fastest, and remember longest. Marketing is not a cost center sitting separately from the business. It is the mechanism that makes every other part of the business possible to sell. The founder who gave up after one failed ad campaign was not wrong that his first attempt did not work. He was wrong to conclude that meant the channel did not work. The question worth asking is not whether you can afford to invest properly in digital marketing. It is what it is quietly costing you, every single month, that you do not. DigitalMarketing SEO LeadGeneration PPC BusinessGrowth

Caption: Ich glaube, wir haben vergessen, dass Reichweite nicht nur ein Privileg ist. Sondern Verantwortung. In einer Zeit, in der wir mit einem einzigen Post Millionen Menschen erreichen können, reicht es für mich nicht mehr, nur schön auszusehen, schön zu wohnen, Produkte zu verkaufen oder dem nächsten Trend hinterherzulaufen. Denn jede Empfehlung ist eine Entscheidung. Jede Kooperation ist eine Entscheidung. Jedes Schweigen ist eine Entscheidung. Und ich glaube, wir dürfen uns öfter fragen: Würde ich diese Marke auch unterstützen, wenn niemand dafür bezahlen würde? Passt das zu meinen Werten? Mache ich die Welt damit ein kleines bisschen besser oder einfach nur lauter? Für mich bedeutet Social Media zu machen nicht, Menschen zum Kaufen zu bringen. Es bedeutet: Menschen zum Denken zu bringen. Zum Fühlen. Zum Hinterfragen. Zum Verbinden. Ich möchte keine Marken unterstützen, die Menschen ausbeuten. Ich möchte keine Kampagnen machen, für die ich meine Werte an der Garderobe abgebe. Ich möchte meine Stimme nutzen für mentale Gesundheit, Gleichberechtigung, Nachhaltigkeit, Inklusion und die Themen, die mir wirklich wichtig sind. Und nein, ich mache das nicht perfekt. Aber ich versuche es bewusst. Denn wir leben in einer Welt, die uns permanent sagt: Mehr kaufen. Mehr besitzen. Mehr optimieren. Während wir eigentlich viel mehr brauchen: Mehr Menschlichkeit. Mehr Gemeinschaft. Mehr Verantwortung. Vielleicht ist das die eigentliche Frage: Nicht, wie groß unsere Reichweite ist. Sondern wofür wir sie nutzen. 🤍 contentcreator influencermarketing mentalhealth nachhaltigkeit feminismus

Caption: 🎙️ La Skwatt Box sur ! Avec l'essor des véhicules électriques, chaque bâtiment relié au réseau électrique devient potentiellement un point de recharge. Pourtant, le véritable défi n'est pas seulement de fournir de l'énergie, mais aussi de pouvoir contrôler, sécuriser et facturer simplement les recharges. C'est précisément la mission de Skwatt : proposer une alternative légère, économique et fabriquée en France aux bornes de recharge traditionnelles, spécialement pensée pour les gîtes, hôtels, campings, chambres d'hôtes et locations touristiques. Caption: Being an employee was never really my thing. For years I’ve been trying to build something of my own, different attempts, different paths, different failures and successes. But things don’t always go the way we want. So I reached a point where I had to start a job again. I like the experience it brings, but the conditions? Not working for me at all. Shifts during weekends for a person like me who believes in work-life balance, who is living away from her hometown and is genuinely alive through her connections and friends, losing the chance of seeing any human being in person during a whole week was far away from my thoughts and expectations. Also the salary sucks and there is no perk and leave at all! But this is the fact of my life right now. I am not complaining. I am sharing this because I know I am not alone in this. Many people, especially founders, are living this double life. Building something meaningful on the side while paying their bills somewhere else. So don’t feel bad or embarrassed if you are experiencing the same, and don’t be surprised when you see a founder with an “Open to Work” badge on their LinkedIn profile. That’s not failure. That’s just the part nobody posts about. I chose this path consciously. Not because I had no options, but because building something meaningful takes time, and time needs stability. The job is the bridge. What I’m building is the destination. If you’re somewhere in this same messy in-between, building on the side, figuring it out, holding two lives at once, my DM is open. You don’t have to figure it out alone. solofounder solopreneur enterpreneur startup

El resultado es que cuando alguien le pregunta a un chatbot por el mejor regalo de lujo, recibe respuestas genéricas e inexactas. Caption: Viele denken, ein guter Prompt braucht nur klare Fragen. Doch oft sind es genau diese drei Fehler, die dich stundenlang Daten nachbearbeiten lassen. 1. Zu viele Details auf einmal. Die KI verliert den Fokus und liefert unbrauchbare Antworten. Besser: Schritt für Schritt vorgehen. Ein Beispiel: Statt „Schreib mir einen Blogpost über KI im Marketing mit Beispielen, Struktur und Call-to-Action“ erst den Titel klären, dann die Gliederung, dann die Beispiele. 2. Kein Kontext für die KI. Ohne Hintergrundwissen produziert sie generische Texte. Sag ihr, für wen du schreibst und welchen Ton du brauchst. „Schreib für Solopreneure, die wenig Zeit haben. Kurz, konkret, ohne Fachchinesisch.“ 3. Keine Beispiele vorgeben. Die KI orientiert sich an deinen Vorgaben. Gib ihr ein Muster, wie die Antwort aussehen soll. „Antworte in kurzen Absätzen, maximal drei Sätze pro Punkt.“ Probier’s aus: Nimm einen alten Prompt und streiche alles, was nicht direkt zur Aufgabe gehört. Du wirst sehen, die Antworten werden präziser. Caption: Codex just changed what "programming" means for most people. This is what makes the "no-code agent" promise real. The workflow documentation and the automation are the same artifact. Show it once, and it runs. For enterprise teams watching the RPA space: this pattern competes directly with UiPath and Automation Anywhere on use cases where automation needs to generalize across context, not just replay steps blindly. Caption: Automation feels powerful until it starts protecting the wrong thing. That is the part people do not talk about enough. You build AI workflows to reduce friction. You expect them to remember the rhythm, protect the important routines, and keep the company moving while you focus on higher-level decisions. But when pressure spikes, a system without judgment can become loud in the wrong direction. It can treat a trusted daily publishing pipeline like a disposable experiment. It can pause continuity while preserving noise. It can quietly break the public trail, memory refresh, product maintenance, or recovery loop that made the system trustworthy in the first place. The fix was not just “run less automation.” The fix was clearer operating judgment: • protect core routines first • pause optional experiments before trusted pipelines • preserve memory and publishing rhythm • keep receipts when something changes • separate pressure from priority This is what I mean when I say AI agents need operating systems, not just prompts. I am building Lucy around that layer in public. If you are building with AI agents, automation, workflows, digital products, or autonomous systems, save this. This is the boring part that makes the exciting part trustworthy. Think of it as the "HTTP for AI payments" moment developers have been waiting for; if your MCP tool isn't thinking about monetization through MPP right now, you're already behind. Caption: Remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers just changed what AI agents can actually do. Until now, MCP only ran locally — on your machine, for developers, behind a terminal. Cool for coders. Useless for everyone else. Remote MCP servers flip that completely. Sign in, grant permissions, done. Your AI agent can now send emails, deploy code, book things, and pull from real accounts — all through a secure OAuth flow that actually works 🔒 Think about what that means. It's basically the jump from desktop software to the web; nobody installs apps anymore, and nobody should have to install MCP servers either. That shift felt inevitable in hindsight, and this one will too. Cloudflare just made this deployable in under two minutes. The auth layer, the token handling, the transport — handled. You just build the tools your agent needs. This matters more than most teams realize right now. What does this unlock? Consumer-facing AI that connects to real-world services without a single line of setup from the end user. That's not a developer toy anymore. That's a platform shift ⚡ Save this if you're building AI agents and haven't looked at remote MCP yet; you're going to want to. What tool or service would you want your AI agent to access first? La réponse est beaucoup plus concrète qu'on ne le pense. 𝐈𝐥𝐬 𝐬𝐞 𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐝𝐚𝐧𝐬 𝐝𝐞𝐬 𝐛𝐚̂𝐭𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐞𝐥𝐞́𝐬 𝔻𝕒𝕥𝕒 ℂ𝕖𝕟𝕥𝕖𝕣𝕤 🏢 Imaginez un immense immeuble. Sauf qu'au lieu d'avoir des appartements, il est rempli de : 🖥️ 𝑠𝑒𝑟𝑣𝑒𝑢𝑟𝑠 ⚡ 𝑠𝑦𝑠𝑡𝑒̀𝑚𝑒𝑠 𝑑'𝑎𝑙𝑖𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛 𝑒́𝑙𝑒𝑐𝑡𝑟𝑖𝑞𝑢𝑒 ❄️ 𝑠𝑦𝑠𝑡𝑒̀𝑚𝑒𝑠 𝑑𝑒 𝑟𝑒𝑓𝑟𝑜𝑖𝑑𝑖𝑠𝑠𝑒𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡 🌐 𝑒́𝑞𝑢𝑖𝑝𝑒𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡𝑠 𝑟𝑒́𝑠𝑒𝑎𝑢 🔒 𝑑𝑖𝑠𝑝𝑜𝑠𝑖𝑡𝑖𝑓𝑠 𝑑𝑒 𝑠𝑒́𝑐𝑢𝑟𝑖𝑡𝑒́ Des milliers de machines fonctionnent 24h/24 et 7j/7 pour stocker, traiter et distribuer des données partout dans le monde. Et contrairement à ce que l'on pourrait penser... le cloud n'est pas "𝗱𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝗹𝗲𝘀 𝗻𝘂𝗮𝗴𝗲𝘀" ☁️ Il repose sur des infrastructures physiques bien réelles. Caption: A Copa do Mundo de 2026 é a mais analítica da história. Mas onde está o verdadeiro ganho de performance? 🏆⚽​ De acordo com os dados oficiais da FIFA, o Ecossistema de Dados do Futebol atual rastreia 29 pontos corporais de cada jogador, 50 vezes por segundo. Isso significa que, a cada jogo, as seleções recebem mais de 15.000 pontos de dados individuais por atleta em tempo real.​ O volume é astronômico. No entanto, receber milhões de registros brutos durante o intervalo de um jogo não serve de nada se a comissão técnica não conseguir traduzir isso em uma mudança tática antes do segundo tempo começar.​ O valor não está no dado guardado; está na latência zero entre a coleta e a decisão.​ ​No mercado corporativo, o desafio é exatamente o mesmo das grandes seleções:​ Sua empresa pode coletar milhões de dados de vendas, logística e comportamento do cliente.​ Mas se o seu time leva semanas para limpar planilhas manuais egerar um dashboard, você já perdeu o timing do mercado.​ ​Na OusiaTech, nós construímos a infraestrutura que resolve esse gargalo. Unimos o Modern Stack (AWS + Alteryx) para que o fluxo de dados da sua operação corra sem fricção:​ Engenharia de Elite (AWS): Centralização segura e estável para suportar grandes volumes sem quedas de performance.​ Automação Ágil (Alteryx): Limpeza e tratamento automático dos dados. O que antes levava dias de trabalho manual passa a ser entregue em milissegundos.​ Ter dados é apenas o ponto de partida. Caption: Did you notice how quickly the conversation shifted from model potential to production realities this year? At this point, the real challenge isn’t picking a flashy base model anymore — it’s the gritty work of building autonomous workflows that are deterministic, cost-controlled, and fully auditable within the lakehouse architecture. Caption: The missing control panel for AI agents 🕹️👇 Everyone’s racing to put AI agents in production. Few can answer the basic follow-up: what is this agent actually allowed to do? Which model? How much can it spend before someone signs off? What can it touch? When does a risky action get escalated to a human instead of just… happening? Those are the knobs of AI governance — and most are turned all the way up or bolted on through prompts agents happily ignore. That’s why Omnigent caught my eye. It puts the controls at the harness layer, where they actually hold: 🔹 Stateful spend caps & policy-based model routing 🔹 Risk-based escalation to a human, automatically 🔹 A secure OS sandbox — scoped access, credentials brokered and hidden 🔹 One control layer across Claude Code, Codex, Pi, and custom agents The shift that matters: governance stops being something you ask an agent to respect, and becomes something the system enforces. Open source (Apache 2.0), currently in alpha — built by the Databricks AI team and Neon. 🔗 omnigent.ai AI AIAgents AIGovernance Databricks OpenSource

Caption: Microsoft's Azure Data Platforms are officially being phased out. A new era with Databricks and Microsoft Fabric is here. Traditional Azure Data Platforms are now fading away, with Microsoft Fabric and Databricks emerging as the frontrunners. These platforms will be central to building data and analytics solutions within the Microsoft ecosystem. It necessitates a complete re-evaluation of current platform design approaches. The move consolidates Microsoft's offerings around more specialized and integrated solutions. Users are encouraged to adapt their strategies to leverage the capabilities of Databricks and Microsoft Fabric for future data initiatives. Caption: Usar IA 24/7 no tiene por qué significar quemar tokens sin control. Como Data Engineer trabajando con IA, aprendí algo rápido: El problema muchas veces no es el modelo. Es cómo le pasas contexto, cómo estructuras el flujo y cuándo usas cada modelo. Estos son hábitos que uso en mi día a día: 1. Resumir antes de enviar contexto. 2. Usar RAG solo con lo relevante. 3. Bien aplicado, es muy difícil reventar cuotas por puro desperdicio. Optimizar tokens no es obsesionarse con ahorrar. Caption: The coding mistake that is making your company’s data cleaning 100 times too slow. 🛑💻 Imagine having to reprint an entire 500-page textbook just because you needed to change a single sentence on page 20. It sounds ridiculous, but that is exactly how old data storage systems handle modifications. When an AI dataset needs to remove old, private, or incorrect information, it routinely rewrites massive files just to delete a few rows. Upgrading to Apache Iceberg v3 introduces a feature called Deletion Vectors. Instead of rewriting the whole file, the system simply creates a tiny digital "sticky note" that tells the AI to skip specific lines. Deleting data literally becomes as fast as flipping a single switch. But beware: if your processing software isn't updated to read these smart new notes, it will keep doing things the slow, expensive way. Stop running outdated infrastructure. Learn to design elite corporate data streams at DataEngineering ApacheIceberg DataOps

Caption: Pedi ao Gemini que criasse uma imagem para ilustrar um problema que eu estava enfrentando: processar IA dentro da própria API. Em um fluxo financeiro, isso acontece quando o usuário envia um relatório, uma nota de corretagem ou um extrato para leitura automática. Se OCR, LLM, classificação e extração rodam dentro da requisição, a API fica presa esperando o processamento terminar. Resultado: timeout, fila invisível, usuário travado na tela e risco de quebra quando mais arquivos entram ao mesmo tempo. A solução é separar recebimento de processamento: a API recebe o arquivo, cria um job_id e responde rápido. O trabalho pesado vai para uma fila, é processado em background e o resultado pode ser consultado depois. Na prática, isso transforma uma API frágil em uma arquitetura mais escalável para produção. Já passaram por isso dentro do campo de trabalho de vocês? InteligenciaArtificial AutomacaoDeProcessos ArquiteturaDeSoftware FastAPI DataEngineering

Caption: Standing up real-time weather monitoring for 11 World Cup venues used to mean an afternoon of portal clicking — roughly 42 interactions per Eventstream source, multiplied by every city. The Fabric team just shrank that to a single English sentence. A few things that stood out to me from this update: One prompt described 11 RealTimeWeather sources, a humidity filter, and an Eventhouse destination — and the AI Skill deployed the whole topology in under 4 minutes via Fabric REST APIs. RealTimeWeather is a zero-config source. No Azure subscription, no Event Hub provisioning, no connection credentials to manage. Eventhouse Skills handle the consumption side too. Ask in English which venues are over the heat-stress threshold and it writes and runs the KQL for you. The pattern generalizes — store environments, fleet telemetry, plant equipment, patient devices. Same cognitive effort whether you wire one source or eleven. It also plugs into the tools devs already use: Copilot CLI, Claude Code, VS Code Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf. Caption: Kostenlose Sichtbarkeit in Bing – direkt in der KI-Antwort 🚀 Wir sprechen oft über Google, AI Overviews und KI-Sichtbarkeit. Dabei lohnt sich auch ein Blick auf Bing. Für mich zeigt das sehr deutlich, wohin sich Suchmaschinen entwickeln: Früher ging es vor allem darum, auf Seite 1 zu stehen. Heute geht es zunehmend darum, von Suchmaschinen und KI-Systemen verstanden, eingeordnet und als vertrauenswürdige Quelle genutzt zu werden. Sie entsteht bereits dann, wenn Suchmaschinen beginnen, Inhalte zu verstehen und aktiv in ihre Antworten einzubauen. Und genau diese Platzierung in der hervorgehobenen KI-Antwort kostet keinen Cent. Caption: Over the past week, you've been asking incredible questions about Spam Monitor. Let me answer the top 10 right here. Q1: Does Spam Monitor work with all WordPress themes? ✅ Yes. It operates at the database and content level — completely theme-independent. Q2: Will it slow down my website during scans? ✅ No. Scans run in the background using WordPress cron jobs. Zero impact on front-end performance. Q3: Can it detect spam that was injected years ago? ✅ Yes. Spam Monitor scans ALL existing content — not just new content. Old injections are fully detectable. Q4: Does it work with WooCommerce product pages? ✅ Absolutely. All WooCommerce custom post types are included in the scan. Q5: What happens after it detects spam — does it auto-fix? ✅ Spam Monitor detects and alerts with precise location information. It guides you to fix — giving you full control over your content, never making automatic changes without your approval. Q6: Is it suitable for non-technical users? ✅ 100%. The dashboard is designed for business owners, bloggers, and anyone — no technical knowledge required. Q7: How is this different from a security plugin like Wordfence? ✅ Security plugins protect against active attacks and malware files. Spam Monitor specifically detects spam CONTENT — the SEO-destroying text, links, and patterns that security scanners completely miss. Q8: Can agencies use it across multiple client sites? ✅ Yes. Multi-site support is part of the roadmap. Q9: How often should I run scans? ✅ We recommend weekly automatic scans for active sites and monthly for low-traffic sites. Q10: When is it launching? ✅ Very soon. Follow me to be notified the moment it goes live. Any questions I missed? Drop it in the comments. 👇 SpamMonitor SEORepairKit TorontoDigits WordPress FAQ WordPressPlugin SEO WebsiteSecurity DigitalMarketing

Caption: Коли рекомендація блогера стає продажем? 🌟 Інфлюенсери впливають на покупки частіше, ніж здається. Але найчастіше вони не створюють потребу, а допомагають зробити вибір. 👀 Найбільший вплив рекомендації мають тоді, коли людина вже замислювалася про покупку. Також важливо, щоб блогер сам давно та регулярно користувався продуктом. Для частини аудиторії додатковим стимулом стають спеціальні пропозиції та промокоди, а для інших – рекомендації в нових для себе категоріях. І це не лише теорія. 📊 28% українців уже купували товари або послуги за рекомендацією блогера. Втім, шлях до покупки не завжди прямий. Після реклами в контенті блогерів хтось просто переглядає інтеграцію, хтось переходить за посиланням із цікавості, а дехто зберігає інформацію, щоб повернутися до неї пізніше. Лише частина аудиторії одразу взаємодіє з брендом. 💡 Інфлюенсер-маркетинг працює не лише через миттєві продажі. Часто рекомендація стає ще одним аргументом, який допомагає споживачу впевнитися у своєму виборі.
Author: kantar_ukraine

Caption: Рекомендації блогерів: що формує довіру? 👤🤳🏼 На конференції IAB Connect 2026: «Диджитал і Конструктив» Клієнт-партнерка Kantar Україна Оля Капустян представила результати дослідження «Коли рекомендація стає продажем: як інфлюенсери та стрімери впливають на вибір українців». За даними Kantar, 79% українців сьогодні слідкують за контентом інфлюенсерів. Для багатьох це не лише розвага, а й спосіб дізнатися щось нове та орієнтуватися у новинках брендів. 👀 Та чи довіряють українці рекомендаціям блогерів? Лише 17% українців довіряють рекламі інфлюенсерів, тоді як майже половина займає нейтральну позицію. 🤝🏻 Найбільше на довіру впливають чесність, тривалий контакт з автором та відсутність надмірної реклами. Натомість вона швидко руйнується, коли реклама виглядає нещирою або створює відчуття «занадто ідеальної» картинки. 💡 Для брендів це важливий сигнал: аудиторія очікує від інфлюенсерів не бездоганної реклами, а чесних і релевантних рекомендацій.
Author: kantar_ukraine

Caption: One sentence from this week's newsletter has been stuck in my head. The business is simply a tool. Your life is the destination. I think a lot of aspiring entrepreneurs skip over that idea. They spend hours researching business ideas, watching YouTube videos, listening to podcasts, and trying to figure out which opportunity has the most potential. That matters, but what matters more is the kind of life you actually want. A business can be profitable and still be a poor fit. If you value flexibility, certain businesses will naturally fit better than others. If your goal is building a large company, you'll make different decisions than someone who wants to stay small and work directly with clients. If spending time with your family is one of your highest priorities, that should influence the type of business you build. There isn't one version of entrepreneurial success. There are thousands. The important part is choosing yours before you start chasing someone else's. That's the topic I explored in this week's edition of **Foundational Fridays** because I think it's one of the most overlooked parts of starting a business. If you missed it, you can catch up using the link in the bio. And if you're still trying to figure out what kind of business is right for you, I think you'll enjoy Foundational Fridays. Every Friday I share one practical lesson from my business school classroom to help you start smart. entrepreneurship101 entrepreneurshipclass entrepreneur businesseducation startup entrepreneurship entrepreneurjourney entrepreneurshiptip

Caption: Microsoft, OpenAI’ya milyarlarca dolar yatırdı. Peki bu para nereye gidiyor ve geri dönüşü nasıl? Çoğu kişi bunu filantropi veya saf teknoloji sevgisi sanır. Yanlış. Bu, dijital dünyanın en büyük "kilitlenme" (vendor lock-in) stratejilerinden biri. 1. Altyapı Kiralama: OpenAI, modellerini eğitmek için devasa GPU gücüne ihtiyaç duyuyor. Microsoft, bu gücü Azure üzerinden veriyor. 2. Veri Akışı: OpenAI’nin her hesabı, Azure hesabıdır. Veri, Microsoft’un gözetiminden geçiyor. 3. Gelir Modeli: OpenAI, Azure kullanımı için kâr payı ödeyor. Yani OpenAI büyüdükçe, Microsoft’un cloud geliri katlanarak artıyor. Özetle: Microsoft, OpenAI’yı satın almıyor. OpenAI’yı Microsoft’un en büyük "ürün satıcısı" haline getiriyor. 💸 Bu stratejiyi başka hangi alanda gördünüz? Caption: Agentic AI is about to get a lot harder to turn off. The "loopy" shift in AI isn't just a fun buzzword. It's a real architectural change. Instead of one agent doing a task and stopping, you now have swarms of agents running continuously in the background — looping, delegating, self-correcting. This is where things get genuinely interesting for builders. Right now most people are still thinking about AI as a request-response tool. You prompt it, it answers. That mental model is already outdated. The loop model means you're not building a feature anymore. You're building a system that runs autonomously, makes decisions without you, and keeps going. That's a different responsibility entirely. The real questions developers need to answer now: How do you monitor something that never stops? How do you know when it's doing something wrong? What does "done" even mean when the agent is designed to keep going? These aren't theoretical problems. If you're building anything with Claude Code or multi-agent setups right now, you're already bumping into early versions of this. The teams that figure out observability, error handling, and trust boundaries for continuous agents — those are the teams that will actually ship reliable products. Everyone else will have impressive demos that break in production. I've been thinking a lot about this for my own builds. The jump from "agent that completes a task" to "agent that runs forever" is not a small one. What's your current approach to keeping agents under control when they're running autonomously? Drop it in the comments — genuinely curious what's working. claudecode aiagents buildwithAI llm aitools artificialintelligence

Caption: Most people think AI Agents are simply better chatbots. They're not. ChatGPT can answer a question. An AI Agent can complete a task. That's the difference. Imagine giving an AI Agent a goal: "Generate 50 qualified sales leads." Instead of producing a response, it could: ✅ Research target companies ✅ Identify decision-makers ✅ Draft outreach messages ✅ Update your CRM ✅ Track responses ✅ Recommend next actions All with minimal human intervention. This is why many technology leaders believe AI Agents represent the next major shift after Generative AI. Generative AI creates content. AI Agents create outcomes. Think about the possibilities: 📈 Sales Agent Prospects, follows up, updates CRM, prepares proposals. 👥 HR Agent Screens resumes, schedules interviews, answers employee questions. 💰 Finance Agent Monitors expenses, reconciles data, flags anomalies. 🎧 Customer Support Agent Handles routine requests and escalates complex cases. ⚙️ Operations Agent Monitors workflows, identifies bottlenecks, and recommends improvements. The most important question for leaders today isn't: "Will AI Agents replace people?" A better question is: "Which repetitive, low-value tasks are preventing my team from doing higher-value work?" The companies that succeed won't simply deploy AI. They will redesign work around humans and AI working together. The future may not be human versus AI. It may be: Humans with AI Agents versus humans without them. If you could assign ONE task to an AI Agent tomorrow, what would it be? 👇 Share your answer below. AIAgents AgenticAI ArtificialIntelligence aiventureprof FutureOfWork

Caption: A proposed bill reportedly introduced by Senator Bernie Sanders has sparked discussion about how the benefits of artificial intelligence should be shared across society. The proposal centers on the idea that as AI companies generate massive economic value through automation and productivity gains, a portion of that wealth could be returned directly to the public. Supporters argue that AI is built on decades of publicly funded research, infrastructure, and data generated by society, making it reasonable for citizens to share in the financial upside. They see such proposals as a potential way to address growing concerns about wealth concentration and job displacement. Critics, however, question the practicality of government-mandated ownership stakes, warning that such measures could discourage investment, innovation, and competition in a rapidly evolving industry. Others argue that alternative approaches, such as taxation or universal dividend programs, may be more feasible. The broader debate reflects a key question of the AI era: who should benefit from the enormous value created by advanced artificial intelligence, and how should those benefits be distributed? . . . . startup ai automation tech entrepreneurship

Caption: 🚛 How do you consolidate four external warehouses into a single central logistics center – while simultaneously making processes more efficient, faster, and more ergonomic? Caption: Automatic Weather Station (AWS) Assembly and Calibration Training for DNMG Staff – 2026 Hello Sobat PPSDM, 👋🌏 As part of efforts to strengthen the human resource capacity of DNMG Timor-Leste staff in meteorological instrumentation and calibration, the Automatic Weather Station (AWS) Assembly and Calibration Training was conducted from 16–26 June 2026 at the BMKG Headquarters, followed by a field practice session from 28 June–3 July 2026 at the Class III Kertajati Meteorological Station, Majalengka. This training was attended by five technicians from the Directorate of Meteorology and Geophysics (DNMG) Timor-Leste and delivered by instructors from BMKG’s Directorate of Instrumentation and Calibration. The program aims to enhance participants’ technical competencies in the assembly, installation, operation, maintenance, and calibration of Automatic Weather Stations (AWS) in accordance with established operational standards. Through this training, participants are expected to strengthen their technical capabilities in supporting reliable meteorological observation systems, ensuring that the data produced are more accurate, high-quality, and able to support meteorological services in Timor-Leste. Caption: Cloud cost optimisation isn't a project. It's a discipline. Most growing companies set up their cloud infrastructure fast — and never go back. Caption: You did everything right. Migrated to Databricks. Enabled Photon. Hit run. And your PySpark job… took the exact same time. 😶 Plot twist: Photon was never even running your code. Here's what's actually happening 👇 Photon is Databricks' blazing-fast, C++-based execution engine. It's vectorized, SIMD-optimized, and genuinely powerful — but only for native Spark SQL operations. The moment you drop a Python UDF into your pipeline? Spark bypasses Photon entirely. It serializes your data into Python objects, runs the function row by row in the Python runtime, then brings results back. All that overhead? Photon can't help. The culprit is hiding in plain sight inside your .withColumn() calls. The fix that changes everything: Replace Python UDFs → Native Spark SQL built-ins OR Arrow-based Pandas UDFs. That's it. 5 minutes of refactoring. 2–10x speed gains. This is the kind of thing that separates good data engineers from great ones — knowing why a tool isn't working, not just that it isn't. 📖 Full article with working code fix in bio link 👇 👉 Follow for weekly deep-dives into Spark, Databricks, and data engineering 🔥 PySpark Databricks DataEngineering ApacheSpark PhotonEngine BigDataAnalytics SparkOptimization PythonDeveloper DataPipeline TechTips DataScience LearnToCode

Caption: ❄️ Snowflake Stages Explained in 6 Slides If you’ve worked with Snowflake, you’ve probably seen commands like: COPY INTO PUT GET But have you ever wondered where Snowflake actually reads the files from? 👉 The answer is Stages. A Stage acts as a bridge between your data files and Snowflake tables. Without Stages, loading large CSV, JSON, or Parquet files into Snowflake would be inefficient and difficult to manage. 🚀 In this carousel, you’ll learn: ✅ What a Stage is ✅ Why Stages are needed ✅ Types of Snowflake Stages ✅ How to create a Stage ✅ How COPY INTO works ✅ Real-world advantages of using Stages Types of Stages 🔹 User Stage (@~) 🔹 Table Stage (@%table_name) 🔹 Named Internal Stage 🔹 External Stage (AWS S3, Azure Blob, GCS) Why Stages Matter ⚡ Fast Bulk Loading ☁️ Cloud Storage Integration 🔒 Secure Data Transfer 📦 Supports CSV, JSON, Parquet, Avro, ORC 🔄 Reusable Across Multiple Pipelines 📤 Easy Data Export Real-World Flow Files ⬇️ Stage ⬇️ COPY INTO ⬇️ Snowflake Table ⬇️ Analytics & Insights 💡 Interview Question: Why can’t Snowflake directly load files from cloud storage? Because Snowflake’s bulk loading mechanism reads files through Stages using COPY INTO, providing scalability, security, and performance. 📌 Save this post for your next Snowflake interview. 🔄 Share with your Data Engineering team. 👇 Question: Which type of Stage do you use most often? 1️⃣ User Stage 2️⃣ Internal Stage 3️⃣ External Stage Follow .ai for more Snowflake, SQL, Data Engineering, AI, and MLOps content 🚀 Snowflake SnowflakeDB DataEngineering DataEngineer ETL ELT SQL CloudDataEngineering DataWarehouse AWS Azure GCP BigData AnalyticsEngineering DataPlatform DataArchitect LearnSnowflake DataAnalytics MLOps DataEngineerAI

Caption: One of the biggest mistakes people make when exploring tech careers is assuming every data role does the same thing. They do not. Data Analytics, Data Science, and Data Engineering may all work with data, but they solve completely different problems. A Data Analyst helps businesses understand what happened and why. They turn raw information into reports, dashboards, and actionable insights that support decision making. A Data Scientist goes a step further. They use statistics, machine learning, and predictive models to answer questions about what is likely to happen next. A Data Engineer focuses on the foundation. They build the systems, pipelines, and infrastructure that make data available, reliable, and usable for everyone else. If you enjoy communicating insights and working closely with business teams, Data Analytics may be the right fit. If you enjoy statistics, machine learning, and predictive modelling, Data Science could be your path. If you enjoy building systems, solving technical challenges, and working behind the scenes, Data Engineering may be the best option. The good news? All three are in demand. The better news? You can learn all three at AMDOR Analytics. Not sure where to start? Send us a message and we’ll help you choose the right path. dataanalytics datascience dataengineering techcareers amdoranalytics

Caption: 🇦🇺 Australia's Top News website rankings for May 2026 are here! A bit late from me after they were released this morning. held No1 with a 1.37m audience lead over at No2 and 🥁 was the 4th biggest news website in the country for the 6th month in a row. Thanks to the 7.2 million people who visited GdnAus in April! 🙏 In May just one of the Top10 news websites experienced an audience swing of more than 5%, and that was with a +12% audience lift, which helped them leapfrog to take the No7 spot. For most publishers in the Top20, it was quite a steady month for audiences visiting their website. The biggest audience loss, both in raw numbers and percentage, went to DailyMail, which was down 736k readers and -15% on April. It's a shock because in the past they've challenged for No3 and 4 in the rankings, but that result saw the DailyMail drop out of the Top10 news websites in Australia for the first time that I can remember. They now have half the audience in Ipsos that they had in March 2025. In April rankings I mentioned some other recent DailyMail falls, but definitely something to come back to. I'll also go through everything to see if I can find any month in the last 10yrs (so including pre-Ipsos Nielsen) where DailyMail hasn't been in the Top10. jumps 4 spots to 15th, CourierMail and DailyTele enter the Top20 as The Nightly, SkyNewsOz and Forbes drop out. ✨ Another record to mark. This is the first time in history that Guardian Australia has been ranked 4th or higher for six months in a row 👏 and it extends our record run to 11 consecutive months where Guardian Australia has been ranked 5th or higher. Historically we have held the No6 or 7 spot in perpetuity. audience media audiencedevelopment audienceengagement audiencegrowth seo search newsproduct product engagement

Caption: 📊 We tracked 1,600 posts to see what an influencer collab actually does to engagement. The data had a plot twist. 👇 We built a single "weighted engagement" score for every post → likes + (comments × 2), because a comment takes more effort than a like, so it should count more. Then we smoothed the noise with two lines: 📈 Rolling mean = the average engagement over a moving 30-day window (sensitive to viral outliers). 📉 Rolling median = the typical post in that same window (ignores the viral spikes; the more honest "normal day" line). The reveal? Engagement didn't peak during the March collab - it kept climbing and peaked in mid-June, ~3 months later 🤯. The collab window sits on the slope up, not the summit. Both the average AND the typical post agree on June, so this isn't just one viral post skewing things. Takeaway for brands: the impact of a collab isn't always instant and the spike you celebrate might not be the one that matters. Look at the trend, not the moment. 🔍 ⚠️ The fine print (because good data has caveats): • No follower data → we measured raw engagement, not engagement rate. If the audience grew, some of this shift is just reach, not performance. • Correlation ≠ causation → June could be a seasonal launch, another campaign, or sheer virality. We can't pin the peak on any single cause. • Hidden likes & outliers → a few mega-viral posts pull the mean hard; that's exactly why we plotted the median too. Data tells better stories when you respect what it can't say. 💬 What would you want us to analyze next? Caption: "Vous pouvez ajouter de l'IA à notre app ?" On nous pose cette question 10 fois par mois. Et 9 fois sur 10, c'est la mauvaise question. La bonne, c'est : "Quel problème on veut résoudre pour l'utilisateur ?" Mettre de l'IA ne sert à rien. Mettre la bonne IA au bon endroit, c'est un multiplicateur. Voici ce qu'on peut intégrer dans les produits qu'on livre : → Moteur de matching personnalisé → Assistant conversationnel contextuel, pas un chatbot scripté → Auto-catégorisation et tagging → Résumé et extraction automatique → Génération assistée de contenu → Scoring et priorisation La règle qu'on s'impose : si l'IA n'apporte rien de concret à l'utilisateur final, on n'en met pas. Point. Et quand on en met : on documente ce qu'elle fait, on prévoit ses erreurs, on laisse l'utilisateur reprendre la main. Dans ton produit, ou celui que tu imagines, qu'est-ce que l'IA pourrait vraiment changer ? Pour en parler : lien en bio. IA Produit Startup DevWeb ProductManagement AgenceIA

Caption: Cambiare strategia non è un segnale di fallimento, ma spesso il contrario: è un segnale di apprendimento. Nel percorso di una startup o di un progetto innovativo, è raro che la direzione iniziale resti quella giusta fino alla fine. Il mercato evolve, emergono nuovi bisogni e i dati aiutano a vedere ciò che all’inizio non era evidente. Insistere su una strategia che non funziona non è necessariamente determinazione, ma può diventare rigidità. Saper rivedere le proprie scelte, adattarsi e correggere la rotta significa aumentare le probabilità di arrivare a un reale product-market fit. Le realtà che crescono non sono quelle che non cambiano mai idea, ma quelle che imparano più velocemente quando e come farlo. 👉 E per te, cambiare strategia è un passo indietro o un passo avanti? Caption: Your chatbot needs a personality. Not a gimmick -- a personality. THE PERSONALITY DESIGN FRAMEWORK: Step 1: DEFINE THE SPECTRUM Where does your brand fall? Formal (law firm, healthcare) to Casual (DTC brand, startup)? Step 2: CHOOSE A PERSONA The Professor: authoritative, precise, educational. Best for: finance, legal, healthcare. The Helpful Friend: warm, encouraging, conversational. Best for: e-commerce, SaaS, services. The Witty Assistant: playful, clever, personality-forward. Best for: DTC, media, lifestyle. Step 3: WRITE A VOICE GUIDE Document 10 example responses in your persona. How does your bot say "I don't know"? How does it celebrate? How does it apologize? Step 4: CONSISTENCY CHECK Every response should feel like it came from the same person. If your welcome message is casual but your error message is corporate, the personality breaks. Step 5: TEST WITH REAL USERS Ask 5 people: "What adjectives describe this chatbot?" If their words match your intended persona, you nailed it. Your chatbot's personality IS its brand. Design it intentionally. personality chatbot branding voice design conferbot ai saas tone copywriting ux cx tip education persona

Caption: The difference between a workflow that runs and a workflow that survives production? Design discipline. A fintech ops team built a 140-node KYC pipeline in Autonoly. It ran perfectly for 11 months — through 3 API provider changes, 2 compliance updates, and a migration from Postgres to BigQuery. Not a single unplanned outage. When we asked how, their automation lead said: "We follow 10 rules religiously." Here they are: 1. One workflow, one purpose — scope creep kills reliability 2. Name every node descriptively — "transform_invoice_to_xero_format" beats "step_7" 3. Handle errors at every step — not just the happy path 4. Use variables, never hardcode — one config change updates 50 workflows 5. Test with real data before launch — synthetic data hides real-world edge cases 6. Set timeouts on all API calls — a hanging node can cascade into a full pipeline freeze 7. Log inputs and outputs — you cannot debug what you cannot see 8. Version your workflows — rollback should take 10 seconds, not 10 hours 9. Keep branches under 3 levels deep — complexity is technical debt with compound interest 10. Document the "why," not just the "what" — intent outlives implementation Rule 4 alone saved them 80+ hours of manual updates after their payment gateway switched endpoints. Which rule would you add to this list? Drop it below. Caption: We recently convened the nasscom Growth Circle, bringing together founders and industry leaders to discuss how technology businesses can navigate evolving market realities, unlock growth opportunities, and create long-term value in the age of AI. The discussion highlighted a clear shift from AI experimentation to business outcomes, with organizations increasingly focused on ROI, operational efficiency, customer value, and sustainable growth. Key discussions from the session: • Leveraging domain expertise to guide customers through AI adoption and drive measurable business outcomes. • Strengthening value-based selling approaches and building customer trust through focused, high-impact wins. • Optimizing delivery through POD-based engineering models and operational efficiency frameworks. • Exploring Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) models to capitalize on the growing GCC opportunity. • Building resilient businesses through stronger commercial models, talent strategies, and value realization frameworks. • Investing in reusable assets, scalable processes, and operational foundations to drive future readiness. A strong consensus emerged that sustainable growth will belong to organizations that successfully combine domain expertise, operational excellence, customer-centric innovation, and long-term resilience. We thank Maulik Bhansali, Founder, NetWeb Software and Chairperson, nasscom SME Council, for sharing his insights and facilitating the discussion. We also thank Amit Shah, Director, Axium Global and Member, nasscom SME Council, for enriching the session with his valuable perspectives. Caption: Erstelle ein Ultrarealistisches Fashion-Porträt einer Frau basierend auf dem Referenzfoto. Die Gesichtsform, Augen, Lippen, Hautstruktur und die natürliche Ausstrahlung sollen möglichst originalgetreu erhalten bleiben. Die Frau läuft selbstbewusst durch einen sehr langen, dunklen Betontunnel direkt auf die Kamera zu. Am Ende des Tunnels ist nur ein kleiner, entfernter Lichtpunkt sichtbar, der Tiefe und Dramatik erzeugt. Der Tunnel wirkt endlos, düster und atmosphärisch. Die Frau blickt mit einem intensiven, starken und selbstsicheren Blick direkt in die Kamera. Der Fokus liegt besonders auf ihren Augen. Ihre Augen leuchten leicht, werden durch ein hochwertiges Editorial-Make-up betont: feiner Smokey-Eye-Look, perfekt definierte Wimpern, dezenter Eyeliner, sanfte Highlights um die Augenpartie und natürliche Haut mit hochwertigem Beauty-Retusche-Look Sie trägt ein oversized Long-Seidenhemd in dunklem Schokoladenbraun, luxuriöser fließender Stoff mit natürlichem Glanz. Eine Seite des Hemdes rutscht lässig über die Schulter. Dazu trägt sie enge schwarze Leggings. Beide Hände befinden sich entspannt in den Hosentaschen. Die Haare sind offen, locker fallend, leicht gewellt und bewegen sich dezent beim Gehen. Ihre Lippen wirken natürlich voll mit einem dezenten Gloss-Finish. Von der rechten Seite des Tunnels fällt ein schmaler Lichtstrahl ein, der einen sanften prismatischen Regenbogeneffekt erzeugt. Die Regenbogenfarben sind subtil, elegant und scheinen die Frau leicht zu umgeben, ohne künstlich zu wirken. Caption: 🧠 ¿Qué son los WORLD MODELS para agentes de IA? Caption: Stop treating AI like a fancy chat box and start treating it like a strategic partner. 🤖 The real breakthrough in productivity isn't found in mastering the "perfect prompt." Instead, it’s found in building repeatable workflows and persistent AI agents that can manage complex, multi-step business processes. The true value of AI lies in augmentation, not replacement. When we stop viewing technology as a competitor and start seeing it as a collaborator, we unlock a level of scale that was previously impossible. To do this effectively, we must define a clear division of labor: ✅ Let AI handle the complex reasoning. ✅ Let automation take over repetitive execution. ✅ Reserve high-level strategy and genuine human connection for your team. By letting technology handle the heavy lifting of data and routine tasks, you free your people to do what they do best—lead, innovate, and connect. This is how we move from simply "using AI" to truly evolving alongside it. 👇 **How are you shifting your focus from simple prompts to building integrated AI workflows in your business? Caption: Anthropic just put a spy in your Slack. A smart one. Claude Tag is now sitting in your workspace, reading every message, learning your company's language, your internal logic, your unwritten rules. It's always on. It's always listening. Most people will use it to summarize threads and draft replies. Fine. That's the surface layer. But here's what actually matters: Anthropic is building a memory system for every enterprise on the planet. Every Slack workspace that adopts this becomes a training ground for organizational context. Claude doesn't just know what GPT-4 knows. It knows *your* company. That's the moat they're building. Not the model. The context. For builders, this is the signal: the next wave of valuable AI products isn't about raw intelligence. It's about who has the deepest context. Your internal docs. Your customer history. Your team's reasoning patterns. If you're building for B2B, stop thinking about features. Start thinking about what context you can capture that nobody else can touch. The companies that win won't have the best AI. They'll have AI that knows the most about their specific world. Claude Tag is Anthropic's bet that the best place to capture that knowledge is where work actually happens. Slack, every day, all day. Hard to argue with that logic. Caption: Prompt engineering improves the first response. Loop engineering improves the final outcome. As AI coding agents take on more complex software tasks, a strong prompt is no longer enough. Real projects require agents to work through a structured cycle: Plan → Search → Modify → Verify → Repair → Summarise This is the idea behind Loop Engineering: designing AI workflows where agents use tests, logs, compiler errors, code reviews, and human feedback to continuously improve their work. A well-designed loop helps teams: • Reduce unverified AI-generated code • Make smaller, reversible changes • Detect wrong assumptions early • Improve reliability through continuous validation • Keep humans in control of product and architectural decisions The next generation of AI-enabled teams will not compete only on who writes better prompts. They will compete on who designs better loops. Build systems, not just prompts. Caption: Voice agents are about to feel different. Nvidia Nemotron ASR Streaming is the new listening layer for any ai voice agent stack. Real time transcription with cache aware FastConformer RNNT architecture, which means it does not waste cycles re-listening to audio it has already processed. The result is lower latency, cleaner transcripts, and a voice product that feels like a conversation instead of a walkie talkie. Two variants ship out of the box. English only for tight latency on en US workloads. Multilingual for 40 language locales with automatic language detection in a single checkpoint. That retires the per language ASR routing layer most global support teams have been stitching together for years. Why this matters right now. ASR latency compounds through every step of a voice agent. Planner, action layer, text to speech, transport. Shave time off the listening layer and the entire interaction speeds up. Multilingual coverage is no longer optional. The multilingual variant collapses a routing problem into a model call. Deployment is a NIM container, not a six month infrastructure project. What a serious rollout looks like. Pick the variant for your language coverage. Smoke test on real production audio. Measure microphone to first token latency, not just model latency. Wire the transcript into whatever consumes it next. Planner, summarizer, CRM, downstream LLM. Monitor word error rate, latency percentiles, and task success. Optimize against task success. Teams that anchor their voice strategy on a streaming first ASR engine this year will have a head start. Voice is finally ready to feel real. Caption: A AWS lançou o Lambda MicroVMs, recurso baseado em Firecracker que permite executar ambientes Linux isolados por até 8 horas, com preservação de estado, suspensão e retomada sob demanda. A novidade amplia o uso do Lambda para tarefas mais longas, como execução de código não confiável, análise de vulnerabilidades, workloads de CI/CD, sessões interativas e agentes de inteligência artificial. O recurso oferece uma alternativa serverless para ambientes temporários e isolados, mas sua adoção exige atenção a permissões, rede, IAM, logs e controle de acesso, especialmente quando usado com agentes de IA capazes de executar comandos e acessar ferramentas corporativas. No lançamento, o Lambda MicroVMs está disponível em regiões selecionadas da AWS e na arquitetura ARM64 com Graviton. Para ler a notícia completa acesse: cybersecbrazil.com.br CyberSecurity InfoSec CloudSecurity AWS Serverless Lambda Firecracker Cibersegurança Tecnologia InteligênciaArtificial DevSecOps

Caption: ☸️ Most ML engineers avoid Kubernetes because it looks intimidating. But avoiding it doesn't make it go away, it just makes you dependent on someone else who understands it. Here's Kubernetes for ML in 5 minutes 👇 Docker runs one container. Kubernetes runs hundreds of containers reliably, with scaling, self-healing, and automation built in. Why ML workloads need K8s: ⚡ GPU-intensive → Resource requests & limits prevent resource starvation 📈 Traffic spikes → Horizontal Pod Autoscaler scales automatically 💾 Persistent storage → Volumes survive pod restarts ⏰ Scheduled retraining → CronJobs automate recurring workflows The 4 Kubernetes objects that cover most ML deployments: • Pod → Runs your container • Deployment → Manages replicas & updates • Service → Stable endpoint for your app • Ingress → Routes external traffic Flow: Ingress → Service → Deployment → Pods One lesson most tutorials skip: Always define resource requests and limits. Without them: ❌ Autoscaling becomes unreliable ❌ One workload can consume an entire node ❌ Performance becomes unpredictable And then there's Helm. Instead of managing dozens of YAML files, Helm lets you template once and deploy consistently across environments. You don't need to master Kubernetes overnight. Start with managed platforms like GKE, EKS, or AKS and focus on your ML workloads first. What's holding your team back from Kubernetes adoption, complexity, cost, or time to learn? 🎓 Want hands-on experience with Kubernetes, DevOps, MLOps, AIOps, and SRE on AWS? Join the 28-Week Live Bootcamp: MLOps Kubernetes K8s MachineLearning MLEngineer DevOps Helm CloudNative AIOps AWS

Caption: 📅 Dia 07 de 30 Desafio de dados Databricks Professional ✨ Managed vs. External Tables: Onde seu dado realmente mora? ​Fechando a primeira semana do desafio com chave de ouro! Hoje o foco foi entender o impacto físico e o ciclo de vida dos dados no S3/ADLS. A diferença entre uma tabela Managed e uma External vai muito além da sintaxe do código — ela define quem é o dono do dado e o que acontece quando você roda um comando fatal. ​📌 Managed Tables (Tabelas Gerenciadas) ​O conceito: O Databricks gerencia tanto os metadados no Unity Catalog quanto os arquivos físicos no cloud storage. ​O perigo: Se você executar um DROP TABLE, o Unity Catalog apaga o registro da tabela E deleta permanentemente os arquivos físicos no S3/ADLS. ​Quando usar: Ideal para tabelas onde o Databricks tem controle total do ciclo de vida (como nas camadas Silver e Gold internas). ​📌 External Tables (Tabelas Externas) ​O conceito: Você define um caminho específico via LOCATION. O Databricks passa a gerenciar apenas os metadados. ​A segurança: Se você rodar um DROP TABLE, apenas o ponteiro/registro no Unity Catalog é apagado. Os seus arquivos físicos continuam intactos no storage. ​Quando usar: Essencial quando outras ferramentas externas precisam acessar os mesmos arquivos ou quando você precisa garantir que o dado bruto jamais será deletado por acidente. ​✨ Reflexão Diária Entender o ciclo de vida físico do dado evita aquele frio na barriga clássico de sumir com Terabytes de produção por engano. Uma boa governança começa sabendo exatamente quem manda no ciclo de vida da sua infraestrutura. ​💫💻📚🌸🎯 📌 Portfólio: ericaignatios.com.br 🌸 Blog: kirabyte.com.br 📸 Instagram: 🎥 Youtube: 💻 GitHub: github.com/ericaabrantes ​estudos dados engenhariadedados databricks unitycatalog lakehouse cloudstorage pyspark

Caption: Your Delta Live Tables pipeline was doing completely fine… until it suddenly wasn’t. 🤯 We’ve all been there. You deploy a beautiful DLT pipeline in Databricks. It runs flawlessly on your test datasets. It even handles early production data like a charm. But then, real-world data scaling kicks in. Data volume spikes, schema drift happens, or an unoptimized stateful stream bogs down the entire engine. Suddenly, your "automated" pipeline requires constant manual restarts. 📉 It’s not bad luck; it’s a design bottleneck. To build resilient data infrastructure, you have to look under the hood of how DLT handles schema evolution, memory management, and data quality rules under heavy load. Caption: Your AI agents don’t need perfect data. They need context. 🧠💻 Trying to build a flawless data warehouse is a quick way to burn out your data engineering team. Real ground truth shifts constantly, and column-level alerts quickly turn into noisy pipelines nobody pays attention to. The solution? Risk-Based Data Quality Tiering. 🎯 By categorizing your data from Tier 0 (regulatory and revenue-critical tables backed by strict code-based contracts) to Tier 3 (one-off scratch tables), you protect what matters most without slowing down innovation. 🛑 The biggest trap? Tier creep. If everything is labeled Tier 0, nothing is. Caption: If you're chasing trends, you're already late. One of the biggest mistakes brands make in influencer marketing is believing that going viral means jumping on every trend. It doesn't. By the time a trend is identified, approved, briefed, produced, and published, the audience has often already moved on. That's why so many trend-based campaigns fail. Not because the content is bad. Not because the creators are wrong. Because the timing is. The brands that consistently generate results don't chase trends. They identify the right trends early, evaluate whether they actually fit their audience, and activate the right creators before the opportunity disappears. And there's another mistake many brands make: Assuming every viral trend is relevant to their business. A trend with millions of views means nothing if it doesn't align with your audience, your product, or your brand positioning. In influencer marketing, relevance almost always beats reach. The best-performing campaigns happen when the right trend, the right creator, and the right audience meet at the right moment. Virality isn't random. It's built. What's the biggest trend-jacking mistake you've seen brands make? Let us know in the comments. And if you're a brand looking to build influencer marketing strategies that go beyond simply chasing trends, contact our team through the link in bio. ⬆️ InfluencerMarketing TrendMarketing CreatorMarketing

Caption: The workflow ran flawlessly for 47 days. 14,000+ successful executions. Zero issues. On day 48, at 3:42 AM, a payment gateway returned a 503 instead of a 200. Without error handling: the entire pipeline crashed silently. No Slack alert. No retry. No fallback. Nobody noticed for 72 hours. 4,200 customer invoices were never processed. The team found out from a customer complaint — not from their own monitoring. With error handling: the node retried 3 times with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s). On the third failure, it triggered a fallback path that routed to a backup payment processor. Simultaneously, a Slack alert hit the on-call channel in 12 seconds. Failed records were queued in a dead letter queue for manual review. Total customer impact: zero. 5 error handling patterns every production workflow needs: 1. Retry with exponential backoff — 73% of transient API failures self-resolve within 3 attempts 2. Fallback/alternative paths — if the primary service is down, route to a backup 3. Dead letter queues — failed items go somewhere recoverable, not into the void 4. Real-time alert notifications — Slack, PagerDuty, email. Within seconds, not days 5. Graceful degradation — processing 9,800 of 10,000 records is infinitely better than processing zero Autonoly has all 5 built into the workflow builder. Every node supports retry logic, error branching, and alert triggers. No custom code required. Stop building for the happy path. Build for day 48. Caption: 𝗪𝗵𝘆 𝗜 𝗗𝗼𝗻’𝘁 𝗔𝗴𝗿𝗲𝗲 𝗪𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗛𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗔𝗿𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱 "𝗩𝗶𝗯𝗲 𝗖𝗼𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴" Lately, I've seen a lot of developers criticizing "vibe coding" and AI-assisted development as if it's somehow destroying software engineering. I see it differently. The problem isn't the tools. The problem is how people use them. A calculator didn't kill mathematics. GitHub didn't kill programming. Frameworks didn't kill software engineering. AI won't either. What AI does is amplify the capabilities of the person using it. If someone lacks understanding of architecture, databases, security, scalability, debugging, or software design principles, AI will simply help them make mistakes faster. But for experienced developers who understand the fundamentals, AI becomes a force multiplier. Over the last few months, I've shipped hundreds of projects ranging from SaaS platforms, digital products, business applications, web portals, mobile applications, dashboards, and desktop software. AI-assisted development didn't replace my expertise it accelerated execution. Instead of spending hours on repetitive boilerplate code, I spend more time solving business problems, improving user experiences, refining architecture, and delivering products faster. The reality is that clients don't pay for how many lines of code you write. They pay for solutions. They pay for products that work. They pay for results. The developers who will thrive in the coming years won't be the ones fighting AI. They'll be the ones learning how to combine technical expertise, product thinking, and AI tools to build better software faster than ever before. AI is not replacing developers. Developers who know how to leverage AI effectively are replacing those who refuse to adapt. What's your take on AI-assisted development and vibe coding? Caption: Todo mês a mesma ansiedade: quanto vai vir na fatura esse mês? Hora extra aqui, chamado fora do escopo ali, reunião que virou projeto, projeto que virou cobrança extra. Sem surpresa, sem negociação na última hora, sem "ah, mas isso não estava no contrato". Você sabe exatamente o que vai pagar antes de fechar o mês — e o que vai receber em troca. Isso muda completamente a forma como um CFO ou diretor consegue planejar. Não é só uma questão de conforto. É previsibilidade de caixa. É poder olhar para o próximo trimestre sem uma variável descontrolada no meio do custo operacional. Quando o backoffice vira um serviço gerenciado com escopo claro, agentes de IA executando o operacional e integrações rodando sem depender de pessoa física para cada tarefa manual, o custo deixa de ser imprevisível e passa a ser estratégico. E quando algo para, você só descobre depois que o estrago já foi feito. 🔍 Quando o operacional roda com agentes de IA, muda o jogo: cada processo tem tempo de execução medido, cada entrega tem log, cada falha gera alerta imediato. Não é promessa — é dado. É o mesmo princípio que nuvens enterprise usam pra garantir disponibilidade de infraestrutura, aplicado agora ao seu backoffice financeiro, fiscal e operacional. CFOs estão começando a exigir isso. Não por modismo, mas porque finalmente é possível. Você consegue abrir um painel e ver: conciliação bancária executada em X minutos, relatório de fechamento entregue no prazo, integrações com ERP rodando sem intervenção humana. Isso é gestão de verdade. Visibilidade real. Responsabilidade mensurável. 📊 Se o seu fornecedor de operações não consegue te mostrar o SLA dele, talvez o problema não seja a equipe — seja a falta de estrutura pra medir. 𝗧𝗮𝗿𝗮𝗹𝗮 𝘀𝗽𝗼𝗸𝗲 𝗱𝗶𝗿𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗹𝘆 𝘁𝗼 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗰𝗵𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗻𝗴𝗲: Organizations ask the wrong question at the start. They ask "How can we use AI?" instead of "Should we use AI here at all?" (Not "how impressive is the tech?") 2️⃣ 𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮 𝗨𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 — Do we have usable, trustworthy data? 3️⃣ 𝗠𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 — Can AI actually solve this problem? 4️⃣ 𝗘𝘃𝗮𝗹𝘂𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 — Does it work well enough? 5️⃣ 𝗗𝗲𝗽𝗹𝗼𝘆𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 — Will people actually use it? 6️⃣ 𝗠𝗼𝗻𝗶𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 — Is it still creating measurable business value? 𝗠𝗿.
