Caption: 홈페이지를 볼 때 생각보다 많이 보이는 구조가 있습니다. 메인페이지 하나에 회사소개, 서비스소개, 장점, 사례, 문의 버튼까지 전부 몰아넣는 방식입니다🫠🫠🫠 처음 보기에는 깔끔해 보일 수 있습니다. 하지만 SEO 관점에서는 이 구조가 검색 의도를 받기 어려울 때가 많습니다. 고객은 생각보다 구체적으로 검색합니다. “SEO 업체”라고 검색하는 사람도 있지만, “홈페이지 SEO 점검” “구글 검색 노출 방법” “블로그 유입 없는 이유” “메타타이틀 설정”처럼 각자 다른 문제와 목적을 가지고 검색합니다. 그런데 모든 내용을 하나의 서비스 페이지에 넣어두면 검색엔진도, 고객도 이 페이지가 정확히 어떤 질문에 답하는지 이해하기 어려워집니다. 서비스 페이지를 쪼개야 하는 이유는 단순히 페이지 수를 늘리기 위해서가 아닙니다↕️↕️↕️ 각 페이지가 각각의 검색 의도를 받을 수 있게 만들기 위해서입니다. 예를 들어 SEO 대행 페이지, 홈페이지 SEO 점검 페이지, 콘텐츠 SEO 페이지, AEO/GEO 대응 페이지, Google Ads 데이터 기반 SEO 페이지가 각각 나뉘어 있으면 고객은 자기 상황에 맞는 답을 더 쉽게 찾을 수 있습니다. 검색엔진도 각 페이지의 주제와 역할을 더 명확하게 이해할 수 있습니다. 좋은 홈페이지는 모든 말을 한 페이지에 다 넣는 구조가 아니라 고객의 검색 의도에 맞게 페이지 역할이 나뉘어 있는 구조입니다. 우리 홈페이지는 지금 서비스를 한 번에 설명하고 있나요, 아니면 고객의 검색 의도별로 답을 나누고 있나요✅️✅️✅️ seo geo aeo diumseo 구글seo

Caption: Du behöver inte ha flest följare för att vara ett starkt val för ett varumärke. Mikro-kreatörer vinner ofta samarbeten av en enkel anledning: de erbjuder relevans, förtroende och innehåll som känns naturligt i flödet. För kreatörer betyder det här: • Sluta be om ursäkt för en mindre publik • Visa hur din nisch matchar rätt målgrupp • Lyft fram signaler som klick, sparningar, svar och tydlig köpsignal • Paketera ditt värde som målgruppsfit + förtroende + användbart innehåll För varumärken betyder det här: • Titta inte bara på räckvidd • En mindre kreatör kan innebära lägre kampanjrisk och bättre relevans • ROI handlar ofta om rimlig och tydlig effekt, inte bara stora volymer Kort sagt: mindre publik betyder inte mindre värde. Ofta tvärtom. I artikeln går vi igenom varför mikro-kreatörer ofta presterar starkt, vad varumärken faktiskt menar med ROI och hur du pitchar din storlek som en fördel. Caption: ⚡ Slabý prompt verzus silný prompt: rozdiel v praxi Pri AI často nerozhoduje nástroj. Rozhoduje zadanie. Veľa ľudí napíše do ChatGPT niečo veľmi všeobecné a potom čaká konkrétny výsledok. Lenže takto to nefunguje. ❌ Slabý prompt: „Napíš mi niečo o mojich službách.“ Výsledok? Často všeobecný text, ktorý môže použiť takmer hocikto. Bez osobitosti. Bez jasného smeru. Bez reálnej hodnoty pre zákazníka. ✅ Silný prompt: „Si marketingový stratég pre malé firmy. Napíš krátky Facebook/Instagram príspevok pre živnostníka, ktorý chce vysvetliť hodnotu svojej služby. Cieľom je budovať dôveru u potenciálnych zákazníkov. Použi jednoduchý, odborný a ľudský tón.“ Rozdiel je obrovský. Silný prompt dá AI: 🎯 presnú úlohu 👥 konkrétneho zákazníka 🧩 jasný problém ✍️ požadovaný štýl 📌 cieľ výstupu A práve preto je výsledok lepší. Nie preto, že AI zrazu „viac vie“. Ale preto, že má lepšie vstupy. 📌 Slabý prompt necháva AI hádať. Silný prompt jej dáva smer. Ak chcete lepšie výstupy, nezačínajte otázkou: „Čo mi vie AI napísať?“ Začnite otázkou: 👉 „Čo presne potrebujem dosiahnuť a aké informácie musí AI dostať, aby mi s tým pomohla?“ Kvalitný výstup nezačína v AI. Caption: 【NEW!!】AI Agents - Shop Clerk Edition We've released our new product — "AI Agents - Shop Clerk Edition"! It's built for one job: greeting and helping customers as a shop clerk. Caption: More autonomy isn't the flex. Bounded autonomy is. The hard part of an AI agent was never the model. It's the boundaries you put around it. Think of an agent as a capable junior with perfect patience and zero institutional knowledge. It'll follow a process tirelessly — but it needs to be told exactly what it can and can't do on its own. The four things that actually make an agent production-ready (none of them are the AI): - Least-privilege access — if it CAN delete records, one day it will - Approval gates — a human signs off before anything costly - Audit logging — every action traceable - A named owner — because workflows drift The agents that blow up in production usually weren't dumb. They were over-permissioned, with no gate and no log. Start with one small lane. Prove it. Then widen it. Save this for your next AI project. . . . Caption: Anthropic thinks Claude might have a tiny inner workspace. Not consciousness. Anthropic was careful about that part. But they found something like 25 active "concepts" inside Claude at any moment, less than 10 percent of everything it's actually doing. Steerable. Reportable. Tied to how it reasons through multi-step problems. Meanwhile the practical news got buried under that headline. Claude Fable 5 shipped June 9 at double Opus 4.8's price. And Anthropic's own guidance says the detailed, step-by-step prompts that worked great on older models can now make Fable 5 perform worse. I run a lot of those detailed prompts for my own Claude Code setup. That line stopped me. The lesson isn't really about Claude. It's that the playbook for last year's model isn't the playbook for this year's model, and most people don't rewrite it until something breaks. Save this if you're still running prompts built for a model you don't use anymore. ClaudeAI AIagents PromptEngineering AItools

Caption: Humanoid robots are becoming more specialized, not every robot is designed to look like a person. Shenzhen-based Moon Dynamics has introduced the L1, a hybrid humanoid that combines legs and wheels to improve mobility and payload capacity for industrial applications. The robot can: 📦 Carry 20 kg on uphill terrain 📏 Reach up to 1.8 meters while carrying loads 💧 Transport containers with minimal spilling thanks to precise force control Instead of relying only on AI trained from data, Moon Dynamics says the L1 also learns from physical principles like torque, center of mass, and acceleration, enabling faster and more stable real-time movement. It's still in the demo stage, but it shows how the next generation of industrial robots may prioritize efficiency over human likeness. Robotics HumanoidRobot Automation IndustrialAI FutureTech

Caption: 🐳 Docker কী? কেন প্রতিটি DevOps Engineer-এর জন্য এটি গুরুত্বপূর্ণ? বর্তমান IT Industry-তে Docker এমন একটি প্রযুক্তি, যা Software Development এবং Deployment-কে করেছে আরও দ্রুত, সহজ এবং নির্ভরযোগ্য। Docker হলো একটি Containerization Platform, যার মাধ্যমে একটি অ্যাপ্লিকেশন এবং তার সকল Dependencies একসাথে Container-এর মধ্যে Run করা যায়। ফলে "আমার কম্পিউটারে চলছে, কিন্তু সার্ভারে চলছে না"—এই সমস্যার সমাধান হয়। 🚀 Docker শিখলে যা করতে পারবেন: ✅ কয়েক সেকেন্ডে Application Deploy করতে পারবেন। ✅ একই Container যেকোনো Server বা Cloud-এ Run করতে পারবেন। ✅ Development, Testing এবং Production Environment একরকম রাখতে পারবেন। ✅ Microservices Architecture নিয়ে কাজ করতে পারবেন। ✅ Kubernetes, CI/CD এবং DevOps Workflow-এর সাথে সহজে Integration করতে পারবেন। 🎯 কারা Docker শিখবেন? ✔️ DevOps Engineer ✔️ Cloud Engineer ✔️ Software Developer ✔️ System Administrator ✔️ Network Engineer ✔️ IT Student ও Fresh Graduate বর্তমানে Docker ছাড়া আধুনিক DevOps এবং Cloud Infrastructure কল্পনা করা কঠিন। AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Kubernetes—সব ক্ষেত্রেই Docker একটি অত্যন্ত গুরুত্বপূর্ণ দক্ষতা। 💼 আপনি যদি আন্তর্জাতিক মানের DevOps বা Cloud Engineer হতে চান, তাহলে Docker শেখা হতে পারে আপনার ক্যারিয়ারের অন্যতম সেরা বিনিয়োগ। 💬 আপনি কি Docker ব্যবহার করেছেন? নাকি এখনই শেখা শুরু করতে চান? কমেন্টে জানান! Caption: 🚧 Bouwvakantie komt eraan! Dit is hét moment voor scheurherstel. Tijdens de bouwvakperiode is het op veel wegen, bedrijventerreinen en parkeerlocaties vaak rustiger dan normaal. Een ideaal moment om onderhoudswerkzaamheden uit te voeren met minimale hinder voor gebruikers en omgeving. Scheuren in asfalt lijken soms onschuldig, maar vormen vaak het begin van grotere problemen. Door regenwater, vorst en verkeersbelasting kunnen kleine scheuren uitgroeien tot kostbare schades en onveilige situaties. Met tijdig scheurherstel: ✅ Voorkom je vervolgschade ✅ Verleng je de levensduur van het asfalt ✅ Beperk je toekomstige onderhoudskosten ✅ Houd je wegen veilig en representatief Onze ervaren ploeg zorgt ervoor dat scheuren snel, duurzaam en vakkundig worden afgedicht. Niet voor niets zijn onze reparatieploegen dagelijks door heel Nederland actief voor gemeenten, aannemers en terreinbeheerders. 💡 Ons advies: meld onderhoudswerkzaamheden vóór de bouwvak aan, zodat deze ingepland kunnen worden vóór de herfst- en winterperiode. Preventief onderhoud vandaag voorkomt grotere problemen morgen. Heb je als wegbeheerder, gemeente of terreinbeheerder schades geconstateerd? Caption: 💢 The most underestimated part of any cloud migration? DNS. Teams spend weeks planning servers, databases, and infrastructure—but a poorly planned DNS cutover can still cause downtime. Before switching production traffic, always check: ✅ DNS hosting provider ✅ TTL values ✅ A, CNAME, MX, TXT, SPF, DKIM & DMARC records ✅ API, admin, staging & CDN subdomains ✅ Rollback plan Lower the TTL in advance, test on a temporary domain, and never change production DNS blindly. ✨ A successful migration is one your users never notice. Caption: 🖥️ 서버를 이해하면 IT 인프라가 보입니다! 🌐 우리가 매일 사용하는 웹사이트, 이메일, 인터넷, 클라우드 서비스는 모두 다양한 서버(Server) 가 협력하여 동작합니다. 서버는 단순한 컴퓨터가 아니라, 각자의 전문 역할을 수행하며 기업의 IT 서비스를 안정적으로 운영하는 핵심 시스템입니다. 이번 인포그래픽에서는 네트워크와 시스템 관리자가 반드시 알아야 할 8가지 핵심 서버 종류와 역할을 한눈에 정리했습니다. ⸻ 🚀 꼭 알아야 할 8가지 핵심 서버 🌐 ① Web Server (웹 서버) * 웹사이트와 웹 애플리케이션 제공 * HTTP/HTTPS 요청 처리 * Apache, Nginx, IIS, PHP, Node.js 등 지원 * SSL/TLS 기반 암호화 통신 제공 📧 ② Email Server (메일 서버) * 이메일 송·수신 및 저장 * SMTP, POP3, IMAP 프로토콜 지원 * 스팸 및 악성메일 차단 * 기업 메일 시스템 운영 🗄️ ③ Database Server (데이터베이스 서버) * 기업 데이터를 안전하게 저장·관리 * SQL 조회 및 트랜잭션 처리 * 데이터 무결성과 보안 보장 * ERP, CRM, 전자상거래 등 핵심 시스템 운영 🌍 ④ DNS Server (DNS 서버) * 도메인 이름을 IP 주소로 변환 * 인터넷 주소 검색 지원 * DNS 캐시를 통한 성능 향상 * 안정적인 네트워크 연결 제공 📁 ⑤ File Server (파일 서버) * 중앙 집중형 파일 저장 * 사용자 권한 기반 접근 제어 * 백업 및 버전 관리 지원 * 조직 내 협업 효율 향상 📤 ⑥ FTP Server (FTP 서버) * 서버와 클라이언트 간 파일 전송 * 대용량 파일 업로드·다운로드 * 소프트웨어 배포 및 백업 활용 * SFTP/FTPS를 활용하면 더욱 안전한 전송 가능 🛡️ ⑦ Web Proxy Server (웹 프록시 서버) * 사용자와 인터넷 사이의 중계 서버 * 웹 캐싱으로 속도 향상 * 웹 필터링 및 접근 제어 * 악성 사이트 차단 및 보안 강화 📡 ⑧ DHCP Server (DHCP 서버) * IP 주소 자동 할당 * 서브넷 마스크, 게이트웨이, DNS 정보 자동 배포 * IP 충돌 방지 * 네트워크 관리 자동화 ⸻ ⭐ 왜 서버가 중요한가? ✔ 안정적인 서비스 운영 ✔ 네트워크 성능 최적화 ✔ 데이터 보호 및 접근 제어 ✔ 중앙 집중형 자원 관리 ✔ 장애 대응 및 서비스 연속성 확보 ✔ 클라우드 및 AI 서비스의 기반 인프라 제공 오늘날 AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform(GCP) 역시 이러한 서버들을 가상화하여 제공하며, 현대적인 클라우드 환경에서는 웹 서버, 데이터베이스 서버, DNS 서버, 파일 서버 등이 유기적으로 연결되어 하나의 서비스 플랫폼을 구성합니다. 💡 네트워크를 배우는 것은 단순히 장비를 배우는 것이 아니라, 서버들이 어떻게 협력하여 하나의 서비스를 완성하는지를 이해하는 과정입니다. 서버의 역할을 이해하면 시스템 아키텍처 설계, 클라우드 구축, DevOps, 정보보안, AI 인프라까지 훨씬 쉽게 접근할 수 있습니다. 📌 저장해 두면 네트워크·클라우드·시스템 관리자·정보보안(ISO/IEC 27001, 27701, 42001)·AWS/Azure/GCP 자격증(AWS SAA, AZ-104, Google ACE) 학습 시 매우 유용한 핵심 정리 자료가 될 것입니다. Caption: Every Amazon order is more than a purchase—it’s a distributed system in motion. The moment you click “Buy Now”, thousands of events begin flowing across multiple services: 📦 Order Validation 💳 Payment Processing 📊 Inventory Reservation 🚚 Fulfillment & Logistics 🔔 Customer Notifications 🤖 Recommendations & Analytics None of this happens sequentially. It’s an event-driven architecture where every service communicates asynchronously, enabling scalability, resilience, and real-time decision-making. What customers experience as a simple click is actually one of the most sophisticated data platforms running behind the scenes. Great systems don’t just process data. They orchestrate millions of decisions every second. This diagram is a high-level educational visualization inspired by common large-scale e-commerce architecture patterns, not Amazon’s internal implementation. Caption: 🚀 SQL Bootcamp 30 | Day 9 🎓 Learn SQL with 🧠 Indexes in SQL – Speed Up Your Queries As databases grow, query performance becomes critical. SQL Indexes help SQL Server find data quickly without scanning the entire table, making your queries faster and more efficient. 📚 What is an Index? An Index is a database object that improves the speed of data retrieval by creating a structured lookup on one or more columns. It mainly improves: SELECT WHERE JOIN ORDER BY GROUP BY ⚡ Types of Indexes 🔹 Clustered Index Stores table data in sorted order. Only one per table. Usually created on the PRIMARY KEY. Best for range queries. 🔹 Non-Clustered Index Stores index separately from table data. Contains pointers to actual rows. Multiple indexes allowed. Ideal for search operations. 🔹 Other SQL Indexes ✅ Unique Index ✅ Composite Index ✅ Filtered Index ✅ Full-Text Index ✅ XML Index ✅ Columnstore Index 💻 See It in Action CREATE CLUSTERED INDEX IX_Employee_ID ON Employee(EmployeeID); CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_Employee_Name ON Employee(EmployeeName); 🌍 Where Is It Used? 🏦 Banking & Financial Systems 🛒 E-Commerce Platforms 📊 Business Intelligence ☁️ Data Warehousing 🔄 ETL & Data Engineering 🏥 Healthcare Applications ⭐ Why Use Indexes? ✔ Faster query execution ✔ Improves JOIN performance ✔ Optimizes WHERE filtering ✔ Speeds up ORDER BY & GROUP BY ✔ Reduces table scans ✔ Improves reporting performance ✔ Better application response time 🚫 Common Mistakes ❌ Creating indexes on every column ❌ Too many indexes affecting INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE ❌ Ignoring fragmented indexes ❌ Creating duplicate indexes ❌ Indexing rarely used columns 💎 Pro SQL Tip Create indexes only on columns frequently used in WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY, and GROUP BY clauses. Monitor and rebuild indexes regularly for optimal performance. 📌 Save this post for future reference. 🚀 Follow for daily SQL lessons, interview questions, and real-world SQL tips. 💬 Comment "SQL" to get the complete SQL Bootcamp 30 Roadmap. Caption: Inside Kafka Architecture: Brokers, Topics, Partitions, and Replication Apache Kafka powers some of the world's largest real-time data platforms by combining scalability, reliability, and high-performance event streaming. Understanding its architecture is the first step toward building resilient distributed systems. Here's a quick breakdown: Brokers: Store data, manage client requests, and coordinate cluster operations. Topics: Organize events into logical streams for producers and consumers. Partitions: Split topics into smaller units, enabling parallel processing and horizontal scalability. Replication: Creates multiple copies of partition data across brokers, ensuring fault tolerance and high availability. Leader & Follower Model: Each partition has one leader handling reads/writes, while followers replicate data for automatic failover. Offsets: Every message receives a unique offset, allowing reliable message tracking and replay. Kafka's distributed design delivers high throughput, durability, low latency, and fault tolerance, making it the preferred platform for event-driven architectures, microservices, IoT, and real-time analytics. Which Kafka concept did you find most challenging when learning, partitions, replication, or consumer groups? Caption: 📌 Сохранения в Instagram — почему они так важны? Когда пользователь нажимает на значок 🔖 "Сохранить", ваш пост попадает в его личную коллекцию. Caption: Stop chasing London. Start winning your street. Google picks the winner in your town based on who looks most active, local, and trusted. Not who spent the most on ads. Here's how you signal "I'm the one" this week: 🥇 Post about a specific local landmark or event. "Popped into [local cafe] for a bacon sarnie between jobs." Google clocks that local relevance. 🥇 Get one new Google review this week. Ask a happy customer while they're stood in front of you. The proximity signal is huge. 🥇 Check-in to your own business listing. Yes, you can. A quick "open as usual" post on Google Business Profile keeps you fresh in the algorithm. 🥇 Engage with 3 local businesses' posts. Comment, share, tag. Google sees your name popping up around town. 🥇 Use your actual town/area name in your first line. Not "the UK". "We're in Leamington Spa" beats "serving customers nationwide." You don't need to be everywhere. You need to be loudest where it matters. Want a hand making your town notice you online? DM me or grab a quote. We make your brand sizzle like bacon. Caption: Die wichtigsten Google Ads Updates der letzten Monate, die ich gerade so beobachte. Die Branche bewegt sich aktuell ziemlich schnell. Auch Customer Match Lists lassen sich mittlerweile sehr einfach an Shopify und gängige CRMs anbinden. Die Integration in Shop- und Datensysteme wird Monat für Monat besser. Caption: La visibilità online sta cambiando. Non si tratta più solo di posizionarsi su Google, ma di essere scelti dalle intelligenze artificiali che oggi generano risposte: ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity e altri sistemi simili. Questo nuovo scenario prende il nome di GEO — Generative Engine Optimization. A differenza della SEO tradizionale, la GEO non punta al ranking nei risultati di ricerca, ma alla possibilità di essere citati come fonte nelle risposte generate dall’AI. In questo contesto, non vince chi è semplicemente “presente online”, ma chi viene riconosciuto come autorevole, chiaro e affidabile. Per aumentare la probabilità di essere selezionati dalle AI, diventano centrali: 🔴contenuti strutturati e facilmente interpretabili 🔴autorevolezza del dominio e del brand 🔴qualità e coerenza delle informazioni pubblicate 🔴presenza di fonti, dati e segnali di affidabilità La GEO non sostituisce la SEO, ma la estende: aggiunge un nuovo livello di visibilità legato ai modelli generativi. 👉 Oggi non basta essere trovati. Bisogna essere scelti. Approfondisci la GEO e le strategie AI Search su: SEO GEO AI DigitalMarketing RankingRoadItalia

Caption: Czy influencer rozwiąże wszystkie problemy komunikacyjne marki? 👀 To zależy, czego od niego oczekujemy. Wiele marek traktuje influencer marketing jak szybki sposób na zwiększenie sprzedaży czy rozpoznawalności. Tymczasem nawet najlepsza współpraca nie zastąpi tego, co marka powinna budować na co dzień. Influencer może sprawić, że więcej osób usłyszy o marce, nie sprawi jednak, że odbiorcy automatycznie jej zaufają. Nie zbuduje jej wyróżnika, nie określi, z czym marka ma być kojarzona. Właśnie dlatego najlepsze efekty osiągają marki, które traktują influencer marketing jako element większej strategii, a nie jedyne działanie promocyjne. Zanim zaczniemy szukać twórców do współpracy, warto odpowiedzieć sobie na kilka pytań: ✔️ Co wyróżnia naszą markę? ✔️ Do kogo chcemy dotrzeć? ✔️ Jakie wartości komunikujemy? ✔️ Co chcemy osiągnąć? Dopiero później warto zastanawiać się, kto pomoże nam opowiedzieć tę historię swoim odbiorcom. 😎 influencermarketing marketing brandcommunication socialmedia foodmarketing

Caption: 🚀🛩️ A college student has gone viral after building a compact carbon fiber turbojet aircraft in his dorm, a project that quickly spread across aerospace and tech circles. Tomas Salvo says that his 5 kg aircraft, called Reaper, is powered by a 250 newton turbojet and allegedly reached speeds of around 500 mph (805 km/h) after roughly five months of development. Those performance claims have not been independently verified, though the build itself has impressed many engineers and hobbyists. The project also caught the attention of Elon Musk, who floated the idea of a supersonic dive from high altitude. Meanwhile, Boom Supersonic CEO Blake Scholl announced a $100,000 prize aimed at the first amateur built RC aircraft to break the sound barrier. If the figures are confirmed, Reaper would surpass existing RC speed records and stand out as one of the most ambitious student engineering builds to date, highlighting how individual projects can reach a level that grabs attention from major aerospace figures. How much of this could realistically scale beyond experimental builds? 🤔 Media: tomas_salvo22 on X ai artificialintelligence aitools aihacks chatgpt

Caption: Despite pioneering many of the breakthroughs that power modern AI, Google was slower than rivals to release consumer facing AI products. Concerns about misinformation, reputational risk, and the impact on its highly profitable search business made the company more cautious than competitors such as OpenAI. While Google focused on developing advanced AI models internally, companies like OpenAI and Anthropic moved quickly to launch public AI assistants, capturing users, developers, and enterprise customers. This allowed competitors to shape public perception and establish an early lead in the generative AI market. Google has since accelerated its AI strategy through Gemini, AI powered Search, and deeper integration across its products. With billions of users, world class AI research, and massive computing infrastructure, the company is now moving aggressively to regain ground in one of the most competitive technology races in history. ‎Sources: Google, Reuters, Alphabet Earnings Google AI Gemini ChatGPT ArtificialIntelligence

Caption: Grounding Pages sind derzeit ein viel diskutiertes Thema in der SEO-Community. Meine Einschätzung: Eine Grounding Page ist kein Pflichtprojekt und kein Wundermittel, sondern ein sinnvoller Baustein, wenn die technische Basis und die klassische SEO bereits sauber aufgestellt sind. Caption: 科大推出全新AI通識課程，合資格的JUPAS新生每年可獲高達10萬元的獎學金，顯示出香港科技大學在推動人工智能教育方面的積極舉措。 根據最新消息，香港科技大學（科大）將於2026年開設一個全新的AI通識課程，旨在增強學生在人工智能領域的知識和技能。該課程不僅涵蓋AI的基本概念，還包括其在各行各業中的應用，讓學生能夠更好地適應未來的職業挑戰。 值得注意的是，參加該課程的合資格JUPAS新生，每年將有機會獲得最高10萬元的獎學金，以支持他們在學術上的追求。這一獎學金計劃無疑將吸引更多有志於投身AI領域的優秀學生報考科大，進一步提升該校的學術聲譽。 科大的這一舉措不僅反映了高等教育對人工智能技術日益重視的趨勢，也顯示出學校對未來人才培養的前瞻性思維。隨著人工智能技術的迅猛發展，社會各界對具備AI知識的人才需求也在增長。科大此舉，無疑將為學生提供更廣闊的發展空間，並為香港的AI生態系統注入新活力。 有專家指出，隨著AI技術的普及，企業對於具備相關技能的人才需求將會持續上升。然而，也有人認為，學校在推廣AI教育的同時，亦需平衡技術與人文素養的培養，以避免學生僅僅成為技術的執行者，而忽略了道德和社會責任。 你對科大的新AI通識課程及其獎學金計劃有何看法？歡迎在下方留言分享你的意見。 香港新聞 港聞 今日香港 時事新聞 HKNews 香港時事 AI教育 人工智能 ChatGPT 科技 HKTech aiacademyhk 創科 —— 🤖 追蹤 學識最新 AI 工具同科技趨勢 📚 AI 教學 · ChatGPT · 創科資訊 📲 IG： ——

Caption: My agents now have a brain. Every decision, every problem, every reason we did it that way. Lose that and six months later you've got no idea why you built it like this. So I built The Brain, my own long-term memory system for coding agents. Here's how it actually works: It's just markdown files on disk. No database schema, no lock-in. Agents write straight into it themselves, using skills that know exactly where each thing belongs. Decisions get a card, not just an outcome. The brief, the options I was given, which one I picked and why. Shown right there in Mission Control, my own UI. Learnings get dropped in too. Post-mortems, techniques, problems solved. The moment we learn something, it goes straight in the folder. Everything's indexed on a vector database. A file watcher runs it through Google's newest embeddings model the second it lands. Multimodal, so video, audio and photos index too, not just text. Files link themselves, Obsidian-style. Drop a text file in and it searches the database for anything related, then adds those as links in the footer automatically. Media gets a sidecar file. Images, video, audio. We transcribe it and write the gist to a markdown sidecar, which gets indexed right alongside the file itself. The result: observational memory, forever. Borrowed the pattern from Mastra, built my own vector brain on top of it. It goes with me, computer to computer, wherever I build next. Full walkthrough on the channel. Building in public, as ever. Want early access to The Brain? Comment BRAIN and I'll add you to the waitlist. AI AIAgents BuildInPublic AgentMemory VectorDatabase Claude Anthropic DevTools SoftwareEngineering AITools KnowledgeManagement

Caption: AI bisa solve masalah rumit, tapi kenapa sering gagal di hal sederhana? Ternyata dia gak punya 'sense of difficulty'. Bayangkan kamu jadi chef baru di dapur restoran mewah. Chef senior bisa lihat pesanan 'pasta sederhana' dan langsung tahu bisa dikerjakan cepat sambil handle 5 pesanan kompleks sekaligus. Tapi chef junior? Dia treat semua pesanan dengan intensitas sama, sampai kehabisan mental energy di tengah jalan. Itu yang terjadi dengan AI agent sekarang—dia gak bisa assess 'task ini sebenarnya simple, aku bisa langsung execute tanpa overthinking'. Paper ini ngomongin kenapa AI perlu 'complexity awareness': kemampuan untuk recognize kapan problem simple vs complex, agar bisa allocate resources dengan smart. Hasilnya? Execution lebih efisien, less hallucination, better decision-making. Kira-kira step apa yang paling sering AI 'overthink'? Share di comments—yang paling sering aku liat adalah verifikasi data sederhana yang jadi 3x lebih panjang. Caption: What if every deployment could be traced back to a single source of truth? That's one of the things I've been learning about while exploring GitOps. Instead of relying on manual deployments or making changes directly in production, GitOps uses Git as the single source of truth for both infrastructure and application deployments. What I find particularly interesting about this approach is that every change is version-controlled, traceable, and easier to reproduce. A typical GitOps workflow might look like this: ❖ A developer opens a pull request. ❖ Changes are reviewed and merged into Git. ❖ GitHub Actions builds and validates the application. ❖ Argo CD synchronises the desired state to the Kubernetes cluster. ❖ Monitoring tools such as Prometheus help detect issues, while rollback strategies can help minimise the impact of failed deployments. As I continue building hands-on projects with GitHub Actions, Kubernetes, Terraform, Docker, and Argo CD, I'm learning that DevOps isn't simply about deploying faster. It's about building systems that are: ❖ Reliable ❖ Repeatable ❖ Secure ❖ Easier to maintain Every new concept I learn reminds me that automation should not only improve speed but also improve confidence in the deployment process. ❖ I'd love to hear from the community: ❖ Have you adopted GitOps in your projects or workplace? ❖ What has been the biggest benefit or challenge you've experienced? ❖ If you haven't explored GitOps yet, what's stopping you from getting started? Let's learn from one another. devops gitops argocd githubactions kubernetes terraform docker aws azure googlecloud cloudcomputing cloudengineer continuouslearning LearningInPublic

Caption: Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy is laying the foundation stone for Amazon's new data centre in Bharat Future City today, a project tied to a Rs 60,000 crore investment commitment. The groundwork goes back to January 2025, when discussions at the World Economic Forum in Davos led to an in-principle agreement between the Telangana government and Amazon. The framework agreement was signed in December 2025 during the Telangana Rising Global Summit. The state has allocated 202 acres in Bharat Future City and 98 acres in Chandanvelly to Amazon, along with incentives worth Rs 125 crore, aimed at developing the Peri-Urban Region. Phase one construction will be completed within 24 months, with remaining work spread over 10 years, strengthening Hyderabad's digital infrastructure for cloud computing and artificial intelligence. AI 봇이 늘면서 정상 자동화와 악성 시도를 가르는 일이 더 중요해졌습니다. 2. 핵심은 암호 서명입니다. 쉽게 말해 요청에 붙는 전자 도장을 보고, 진짜 봇인지 대조하는 방식입니다. 3. IP 주소 허용목록만으로는 부족합니다. 많은 작업이 같은 주소를 나눠 쓰고, 공격자는 user agent를 흉내 낼 수 있습니다. 4. 봇 운영자는 공개키를 올립니다. AWS 웹 방화벽은 그 키로 요청 서명을 확인하고, 확인 상태를 꼬리표로 남깁니다. 5. 회사는 그 꼬리표를 보고 허용, 제한, 차단을 정할 수 있습니다. 공유 플랫폼에서도 출처를 더 또렷하게 볼 수 있습니다. 6. 단, AWS 문서도 분류가 항상 맞지는 않을 수 있다고 적습니다. 실제 적용 전에는 로그와 규칙을 먼저 확인해야 합니다. 7. 한 줄로 말하면, AI 봇 관리가 ‘주소를 믿는 방식’에서 ‘신분을 확인하는 방식’으로 옮겨가고 있습니다. 테크뉴스 AI보안 AWS 웹방화벽 봇관리 클라우드보안

Caption: When we built Auraa, our data activation platform on Databricks, we hit an engineering wall that many enterprise data teams face. Databricks Synced Tables simply could not support the performance demands of tracking complex governance tables for autonomous AI agents. Traditional approaches suggested spinning up a secondary database master to handle the load. However, introducing a second master would have created data synchronization lag, increased architecture complexity, and complicated compliance. We decided to implement a single, dedicated Governance Writer, which would keep data reads fast and lightweight for active AI agents, while maintaining a single source of truth inside the Databricks Unity Catalog. In his latest perspective, Alan Dennis, our VP of AI and Data Innovation, throws light on the architecture of Auraa and explains how it helps enterprises move from disconnected data to governed, automated execution. It covers the key architectural layers that make enterprise AI practical, including data integration, governance, orchestration, knowledge retrieval, and secure agent execution. Read the perspective to see how a well-designed data platform can help AI agents deliver consistent, reliable business outcomes. Caption: 🚀 SQL Bootcamp 30 | Day 10 🎓 Learn SQL with 🧠 Aggregate Functions in SQL Aggregate Functions help you perform calculations on a group of rows and return a single result. They are widely used in reporting, dashboards, analytics, and real-world business applications. 🔹 Common Aggregate Functions COUNT() Returns the total number of rows or non-NULL values. SUM() Returns the total of a numeric column. AVG() Calculates the average value of a numeric column. MIN() Returns the smallest value in a column. MAX() Returns the largest value in a column. ✍️ Rules for Aggregate Functions ✔ Return a single value for a set of rows. ✔ Ignore NULL values (except COUNT(*)). ✔ Mostly used with GROUP BY for grouped calculations. ✔ HAVING is used to filter aggregated results. ✔ Work best for reporting and data analysis. 💻 See It in Action SELECT COUNT(*) AS TotalOrders, SUM(OrderAmount) AS TotalSales, AVG(OrderAmount) AS AverageOrder, MIN(OrderAmount) AS LowestOrder, MAX(OrderAmount) AS HighestOrder FROM Orders; 🌍 Where Are They Used? 📊 Business Intelligence & Reporting 💰 Banking & Financial Analytics 📦 Sales & Inventory Management 📈 Performance Dashboards 🏥 Healthcare Analytics ☁️ Data Warehousing & ETL Pipelines ⭐ Why Should You Use Aggregate Functions? ✔ Generate business reports instantly ✔ Analyze large datasets efficiently ✔ Calculate totals, averages, minimums, and maximums ✔ Measure KPIs and business performance ✔ Simplify decision-making with summarized data 🚫 Common Mistakes ❌ Using aggregate functions without GROUP BY when selecting non-aggregated columns ❌ Expecting COUNT(column) to count NULL values ❌ Using WHERE instead of HAVING for aggregate filtering ❌ Applying SUM() or AVG() to non-numeric columns 💎 Pro SQL Tip Use WHERE to filter rows before aggregation and HAVING to filter results after aggregation. 📌 Save this post for future reference. 🚀 Follow for daily SQL lessons, interview questions, and real-world SQL tips. 💬 Comment "SQL" to get the complete SQL Bootcamp 30 Roadmap. data SQLBootcamp30 LearnSQL SQL AggregateFunctions COUNT SUM AVG MIN MAX SQL DataEngineering

Caption: 🏥 Can AI reduce paperwork without replacing healthcare workers? A New York hospital is facing criticism after 12 nurses were laid off, with their union alleging that AI-powered software has taken over administrative tasks previously handled by experienced staff. The hospital says the technology is being used for non-clinical paperwork and aims to improve efficiency, while the union argues the layoffs violate a recently negotiated contract and could ultimately affect patient care. The debate highlights a growing challenge across healthcare. AI has the potential to reduce repetitive administrative work, streamline insurance documentation, and help clinicians spend more time with patients. But many healthcare professionals believe these tools should support nurses—not replace them. As AI continues to enter hospitals, the key question isn’t whether the technology should be used, but how it can be implemented responsibly while protecting jobs, maintaining transparency, and ensuring patient safety remains the top priority. 💬 Should AI in healthcare be limited to assisting medical professionals, or is replacing certain administrative roles inevitable? . . . . startup ai automation tech entrepreneurship

Caption: Mobile app idea to a working MVP. 9 steps. Most founders skip step one. Then wonder why nobody uses it. 1. Nail the one problem. Talk to 10 real people who have it. If they do not care, the idea changes now, not after you spend the money. 2. Cut to one core feature. A good MVP does one job really well. Everything else goes on the later list. 3. Map the happy path. Sketch only the screens from opening the app to getting the value. Fewest taps possible. 4. Pick the right stack. Flutter or React Native gets you on iOS and Android from one codebase. Choose for where you are going, not just today. 5. Keep the UI clean. It does not need to be fancy. One primary action per screen. Clean makes early products feel real. 6. Build in short cycles. Core feature working end to end before anything else. Rough and shipped beats perfect and unfinished. 7. Test on real devices. Simulators lie. TestFlight and Play testing. Watch people get stuck without helping them. 8. Soft launch small. A limited group first, not the whole world. Real usage shows what is actually broken. 9. Measure, then improve. Do they finish the core action. Do they come back. Let that decide what you build next. Solve one thing well. Ship it fast. Let real users pull the roadmap out of you. That is the difference between an MVP that just exists and one that actually works. Want a partner for all nine steps? Book a call with me and we will map your MVP out together. It is free, there is no pressure, and you leave with a clear plan either way. The link is in my bio. Which step are you stuck on right now? Tell me below. Follow for more. mvp mobileapp startup founders appdevelopment

Caption: Fahrzeit oder Arbeitszeit? Klingt erstmal nach Detailfrage, sorgt in der Praxis aber regelmäßig für Diskussionen. Wann beginnt der Arbeitstag? Was zählt zur Arbeitszeit? Wie werden Fahrten zum Kunden, Projektwechsel oder Pausen sauber dokumentiert? Und warum fällt das meistens genau dann auf, wenn gerade niemand Zeit für Rückfragen hat? Gerade bei mobilen Teams, Baustellen, Objekten oder Außendienst-Terminen ist klar: Wer unterwegs arbeitet, braucht mehr als Bauchgefühl und Zettelwirtschaft. Denn wenn Start, Wechsel, Pause und Feierabend nicht sauber erfasst werden, wird aus einem normalen Arbeitstag schnell ein kleines Abrechnungsrätsel. Mit MomoZeit wird der Ablauf digital, klar und nachvollziehbar dokumentiert — vom Check-in bis zum Feierabend. Klare Erfassung statt Diskussion. Ihr wollt prüfen, wie ihr Fahrzeiten und Arbeitszeiten bei euch praktisch, effizient und alltagstauglich erfassen könnt? Dann meldet euch gerne bei uns — wir schauen gemeinsam, wie sich das sauber umsetzen lässt. Hinweis: Ob Fahrzeit als Arbeitszeit gilt, hängt vom Einzelfall ab, z. B. von Einsatzort, Vereinbarung und Tätigkeit. Eine saubere Dokumentation hilft, die Abläufe nachvollziehbar zu machen. Caption: The premium for a studio photoshoot of a single luxury candle or skincare line often exceeds the product's actual margin. It shouldn't. For beauty and home brands, selling a product is often about selling an experience, a mood, or an aspiration. This means moving beyond sterile product shots to rich, contextual lifestyle imagery. Traditionally, achieving this diversity for a large catalog involved significant expense: booking studios, styling multiple sets, hiring models, and managing complex logistics. But what if you could iterate on hundreds of visual concepts for a single SKU in minutes? Imagine showcasing a decorative vase in a minimalist urban loft, a bohemian beach house, and a classic study – all from one original image. This shift from physical set builds to agentic AI-driven media generation isn't just about cost savings; it's about unlocking creative agility and market responsiveness. It enables brands to tell every possible story their products embody, virtually and at speed.
